description: Mental Manipulation 89 A pair of headsets, one of which is worn by the user and the other on the subject. If the user has an ADEI or MPCI, it connects to the implant. When active, the subject’s thoughts and memories are experienced di- rectly by the user. The difficulty decreases by 1 for an unconscious subject and 2 for a subject who is under the influence of truth drugs. Though the main use is vulgar, a coincidental use connects the Probe to a computer and then acts as a lie detector. Naturally, the subject must be conscious for this use. Golden Bands

name: Endless Ammo Clips
description: Not actually endless, these clips can hold 50 bullets instead of the usual seven, for heavy pistols. Any time the gun is fired, Arete is rolled against difficulty 5. On Weapons 143 a failure, the gun jams. Energy Enhancement Module

name: Enhancement
description: Nanotech altered skin-cells, this armor is protective but non-obvious. For every two points spent, the oper- ative gains one die of armor that can soak against any damage. It must be repaired and maintained between missions and is useless after a week of sustained vio- lence. It may be bought as a biomod, but costs two extra points and can regenerate itself when the owner eats a high iron, high calcium diet. Body-Forger's Arm

name: Enlightened Smartphone
description: Technocratic hardware and software capable of sup- porting all manner of Enlightened Procedures (often referred to now as Apps), the Enlightened Smartphone is rapidly becoming an essential item in the toolkit of the New World Order, and slowly spreading to other Conventions. For additional background points, the battery can hold more points of Quintessence, any of which can be spent on any Procedure using the Smartphone. However, if the battery is drained, the phone is dead until recharged. Holographic Projector

name: Espiritus Mini-Vac, The
description: After being harassed by spirits during a ritual, a Dreamspeaker took a standard mini-vac and consecrat- ed it, including constructing special cotton and cactus- root fiber filters. Once finished, the spirits continued their harassment and so the Mini-Vac was turned on them. The Mini-Vac can suck up and trap minor spirits. The spirits may be trapped, but their influence can be felt while they are contained (i.e., a Bane will still cause nightmares nearby). The Mini-Vac rolls Arete against the spirit’s Willpower to trap them, difficulty 6, and can hold up to four spirits without being cleaned out. Great Sigil Pendant

name: Ether Tracking Clockwork Wonder Globe
description: Created by Professor Gunderlee, the Wonder Globe is a peacock blue glass and brass model of the Earth, showing in real time the flow of ether and magickal events. It can even rewind and fast-forward this flow, showing shallowings and Nodes, and marks the Nodes in colors: gold for general Nodes, silver for Techno- cratic ones, green for tainted and rose for those conse- crated to spirits by shapeshifters. A simple Arete roll (difficulty 7) is needed to operate the Globe, otherwise it is not clear enough for them to make it out. When the Globe produces paradox, it is randomly distributed to a Mage within 35 feet of it. Paradox Stone

name: Exo-Musculature and Exo-Skeletons
description: Iteration X doesn’t put all of its hardware on the in- side of the body (despite some popular misconcep- tions). Some of it is in the form of powerful exo- skeletons and exe-musculature to enhance field opera- tives. The Exo-Musculature (model “Muscleboy”) gives the wearer +2 Strength, +1 Dexterity, +2 Stamina, 2 dice of armor, 1 die of countermagick and 6 Health Levels. The more powerful “Transformer” Exo- Skeleton gives +4 Strength, +4 Stamina, 8 dice of ar- mor, 3 dice of countermagick and 6 Health Levels. These both come with three points of permanent Par- adox, as they are installations, not suits. Extrasensory Access

name: Fan of Kang Wu
description: Created by a troublemaker to conceal his weaponry, this fan has three effects built into it: • Matter 3: It can transform into any weapon • Forces 2: It can generate attractive or repulsive magnetic fields • Prime 1: It can detect Quintessence Its creator used it to change weapons during combat to keep his enemies off guard and to detect enemy wonders and remove them from the fight with the magnetic field. Fireball Pearl

name: Fishbowl of Prosperity
description: Thanks to the coincidence that the Chinese word for fish and for affluence are homonyms, fishbowls are often treated as good luck charms and fish represent wealth and prosperity. This Fishbowl, when filled with well-cared for fish, acts as a good luck charm in busi- 94 Mystic Armory A tripod and transmitter device that makes onlook- ers perceive the wearer of the transmitter as being where the tripod is. It can work over a range of about 25 yards. For anyone who has the ability to trace an electronic signal, it is relatively easy to trace the signal to the tripod back to the source. Charms ness dealings, adding one success to the side of the person negotiating for the household in any business arrangement. However, if placed in a location with bad Feng Shui, it subtracts two successes instead. Fortune Tiki

name: Flesh Canvas
description: A form-fitting bodysuit of biomatter that the user puts on and it melds with their flesh. The result: they can look like anyone whose DNA has been sampled by the suit (including just by a touch). This can change body proportions and, sometimes, sex, but not height and some other size-based traits. The Mask Maker

name: Girdle of Hippolyta
description: A simple silver-link belt which, when worn, grants near-perfect unity between the mind and the body. It allows the wearer to perform incredible physical feats and to gain strange insights into the motivations and thoughts of those around them. Innovation, Inc.'s Personal Lift Generator 5000

name: Golden Walnut
description: A large, amber walnut. When held and focused on, the user can cause nearby plants to grow quickly and blossom. The Infernal Mole-Blower

name: Hazelnuts of Wisdom
description: Often used as foci, the Hazelnuts of Wisdom grow on a sacred tree deep in the Umbra, whose location has long been lost. As a focus, it gives a +1 bonus to Wits dice pools, if eaten it gives a +2 bonus to Wits for the scene. Plutonium Pill

name: Healing Figurine
description: Non-Puncturing Injector

name: HEAT Chip
description: The Heisenberg Electron Array Trinary Processor Chip exploits the uncertainty principle to pull Trinary levels of performance out of transistors made up of single atom. Unfortunately, due to both containment and heat, these transistors require buffers between them which prevent the chip from getting even small- er. Even so, the chip runs at about ten times the speed of a regular CPU. After two hours of use, however, the HEAT chip will overheat and shut down, requiring significant repair before it can be used again. Holocomputer

name: Heihou no Sebrio The Samurai Suit
description: Developed by Japanese Void Engineers as an alterna- tive to more traditional hard suits, the Samurai Suit is much sleeker and less focused on pure physical strength than is the norm. It jacks into the pilot’s brain for direct control. The suit stands a total of three me- ters tall, and all of them contain a 5.56mm anti- infantry weapon on the left shoulder, an auto-cannon on the right arm, a battle claw on the left hand, and the suit’s sheer Strength, which though not as high as some hard suits, is still significant. The suit uses Arete for sensors (Sphere 1 effects) and for its first appearance in a scene (being vulgar, diffi- culty is 8 or 9 depending on witnesses, or else it just seizes up and fails to operate). The suit burns a full point of Quintessence every turn to operate, and shuts down when it runs out. An unfortunate side effect is that once a person has been made capable of piloting a Samurai Suit, their Samurai Suit Pilot biology becomes inextricably tied to it, and they can- not leave it. Heimdall V Leadstopper

name: Heimdall V Leadstopper
description: Too expensive for mass deployment, the Heimdall V is heterodiamond-palladium glass chainmail, and one vest is on display in the Hamburg Construct where it was designed. There are a few dozen more in existence, but that’s it. It gives four dice of armor with no reduc- tion in Dexterity and can be concealed easily, only being as thick as about two shirts. It can also be paint- ed, but maintains a slight sheen, like technical athletics gear. Impact Armor

name: Helix Ring
description: Triggered by a flare-up in the Ascension War, the Traditions and the Technocracy competing for a sud- den large pool of the newly Awakened, Jangler, a Ver- bena healer, pushed his Technomagickal pod into use before working all the bugs out. As a consequence, it is thoroughly field tested, but the vulgarity of its healing abilities causes it to be unreliable. An Arete roll is made, difficulty 7 (8 with Sleeper witnesses), and on a Life and Nature 75 success it takes an hour to heal the person inside it fully, leaves a few scars, and the patient must rest of several hours. On a failure it causes intense pain, but no injury. A botch, however, causes 5 Aggravated damage to the person inside the pod, and coming from Paradox makes it even deadlier than other things, this is usually enough to kill anyone who is injured enough to be a patient. Each activation costs two Quintessence and requires an Intelligence + Technology roll (difficulty 8). If failed, this increases the difficulty of the Arete roll by 2. Martian Purifier

name: Helm of Heimdall
description: It appears to be the helmet from what is certainly overpriced and unnecessarily ornate tournament ar- mor. However, it is actually intended for an artillery captain. Inside of the visor are crystals and gems ar- rayed into lenses, controlled by manipulating some of the ornate bits on the helmet. The Helm allows the wearer to perceive any object, regardless of distance, in its entirety. Furthermore, it allows the wearer to calcu- late its weaknesses and strengths. This is particularly useful when looking at a fortress or other defensive position. Looking Glass

name: Hephaistos' Tables
description: Three legged tables incorporating clockwork that can move the tables under their own power, carrying large loads across flat floors. Penance Bonds

name: Hermes's Carriage
description: A carriage able to hold five people comfortably or up to ten less so. It requires two drivers, and is able to get places more quickly than other means of conveyance: spending a point of Quintessence allows it to use the Shortcut rote. Hot-Mach I Speedster

name: Hiroshima Bone
description: Often, Mages can involve themselves in ghoulish things. One notable one is the use of the bones of victims of the atomic bombing of Hiroshima. Many of these bones have been imbued with magick due to the sheer horror and impact of how they died, and these have two powers. The first is that, when used as an instrument, a Hiroshima Bone makes it easier to communicate with and otherwise interact with the dead, though they often become more volatile when it is used. Each Quintessence spent adds a success to and such effect. The second is that it can be used as a po- tent weapon. When held during a Melee, for a cost of two Quintessence, an additional damage is dealt to anyone the Mage strikes. Their nature, however, means that Hiroshima Bones cannot be recharged, and when their Quintessence store reaches zero, they crumble into dust. Ghost-Devouring Jack-o'-Lantern

name: Holdout Compartment
description: A cloaked cavity buried in a limb. Anything stored within it looks to all mundane scanning as part of the Scientist’s organic material, only Enlightened tech- niques can penetrate this. Hypermed Injection System

name: Holographic Projector
description: The machine itself consists of three boxes, each of which is a cube a foot on each side. When activated, a holographic copy of a subject with an identical box is generated. This costs one Quintessence per hour and is used sometimes when a phone call just isn’t good enough. For one Quintessence per turn, the subject can fully manifest: capable of interacting directly with the area, and fully. Molecular Phone

name: Holy Water
description: Consecrated in a manner that varies by the religion of the Chorister, holy water is a common tool to many faiths, though most famously Catholicism. For each background point spent, Holy Water will grant one person one dot of True Faith for the duration of a scene. Additional points may be spent to temporarily grant Iron Will, Lucky or Code of Honor. Creatures that are deliberately evil take one point of Aggravated damage per dot of Holy Water if it is used on them. Holy Water is a finicky charm, and those who use it just for a quick power upgrade or who regularly violate the tenets of the religion of the one who consecrated it will often find that it no longer gives them any benefit. Huaca

name: Horatius's Thunder
description: An early firearm, this is a wheel-lock with five bar- rels. The shots are iron balls filled with sulfur that burst into flame as they’re fired. In addition to burn- ing flesh, these can start fires when they hit flammable objects. Silver and cold iron balls are also known for use against enemies that require them, as are balls blessed by priests. A shot is a Forces 3/Prime 2 effect, and cause Aggravated damage, even before the special- ized ammunition against more difficult foes. Each shot costs one Quintessence, and recharging requires the acquisition or creation of more ammunition. It needs to be reloaded after five shots, but reloading takes several minutes for the barrels to cool sufficiently. Huginn and Muninn Suppression Systems

name: Horizon Gateway
description: This is a stable gateway connecting two locations, and in fact causing them to locally merge into a single place. They are always hidden and guarded, and usual- ly include a Mind effect to discourage people from noticing them if they aren’t supposed to. Lady Wudlowe's Menhirs

name: Hot-Mach I Speedster
description: Built by a Cultist trying to experience extremes of speed, the Hot-Mach I Speedster is one of the fastest vehicles on the road. By default it avoids radar (+4 difficulty to all electronic detection attempts) and traf- fic lights change to give it right of way. An Arete roll is only necessary when increasing the speed. When work- ing, the Speedster has a safe speed of 130 mph, a max speed of 230 mph and a maneuverability of 10. Inhabited Car

name: Huginn and Muninn Suppression Systems
description: The Huginn fits the Mjollnir V and the Muninn fits the THOMAS II, but they both work very similarly. They suppress muzzle flash and firing noise without softening the impact. Each attack costs one point of Quintessence. Ionic Disruptor

name: Hydrogen Battery
description: The most common Technocratic Matrix, the Hydro- gen Battery provides a large supply of stable Quintes- sence and can interface with most Technocratic Devic- es and Inventions. Kismet Bindi

name: Hyperphoto Zoom Lens with Spirit Film, The
source_book: Tradition Book: Sons of Ether (1st Edition)
description: An Etherite named Dr. Rotham created this for a Cultist photographer he was friends with. It is an enormous telephoto lens that fits any 35 mm camera. It can take photographs far farther than normal lenses, due to a Correspondence 2 effect built into it. Fur- thermore, with the use of Spirit Film, the lens can be used to take photographs of the Umbra. Rimbaud's Recipe for Sacred Absinthe

name: Imperial Tiger
description: Organic steel tigers created by the Taiping Tianguo. Imperial Tigers are often used as guards, and they can shift into many forms, usually disguising themselves as pieces of metal scenery or small machines. Jade chips (rather than silicon) power their rudimentary artificial intelligences, allowing them to work on principles slightly different from that of more mainstream com- puters. A tiger has 10 health levels, takes no wound penal- ties, had 5 in all Physical Attributes, and has steel claws that do Strength + 4 Lethal damage. Khalil aba-Malek, The Iron Satan

name: Implant Chain Gun
description: The most famous weapon of the HIT Marks, a belt- fed mini-gun extends from a concealed compartment in the cyborg’s back, and then a stream of metal slugs is fired at their target. An Arete roll is used to ready the device, and the agent uses Dexterity + Firearms to hit their enemies. This roll is difficulty 7 and does 8 damage with a range of 150 and a rate of 3. The weap- on can only fire in spray burst and full auto, and each activation and deactivation costs one Quintessence. Implant Radio

name: Implant Radio
description: With sensors in the user’s throat, this body-heat powered implant can pick up subvocalizations in order to communicate over radio frequencies and act as a cellular phone. It has a range of about 10 miles and a GPS that allows anyone broadcasting with an Implant Radio to be quickly and easily located. Implanted Plasma Cannon Technocracy: Iteration X page 52 (as Firearm)

name: The Infernal Mole-Blower
source_book: Tradition Book: Sons of Ether (1st Edition)
description: A steel box with gears, a gas engine and holes in the sides for inserting road flares, this device is designed for driving venomous animals from their lairs. It cre- ates noise and smoke, directing it at the lair of the animals to be driven out. Pahu Ino-Nui

name: Infinite Change Purse
description: Running on a simple Matter/Prime effect, these coin purses, drawstring bags and small wallets allow the Mage to draw forth small change whenever they need it, though never an amount that will exceed Resources 1. It does, however, have the following restrictions: • It can only produce coins of a type that have been put into it before Improved It is extremely common for Mages to take their In- struments and improve them. Sometimes this takes the form of just perfecting them so that they do what they do better. Other times it is enchanting them, so that the Mage always has a wonder on hand when carrying their basic equipment. The Adze Unparalleled 36 Mystic Armory • Some amount of the change of any transaction must be put into it • If it is ever visibly emptied (turned upside down or inside out) it loses its magick. Rod of Holy Cleansing

name: Info Spider
description: A palm-sized computer (or smartphone), with inter- net access and a digital camera. Inside is a minor in- formation spirit that can be sent to retrieve all forms of electronic communications that match a given pattern. This has the downside of often retrieving far too much information due to a poorly phrased query. To perform any search, the Gnosis of the device is rolled (difficulty 5) and it uses the Correspondence Range chart on page 504 of Mage: The Ascension 26 Mystic Armory 20th Anniversary Edition. On success, the fetish finds all the information that is non-encrypted that satisfies the query. If there are no additional successes, this takes 2-3 days, one more success gets it in 12 hours, two cause it to take 3 hours and three or more success- es cause it to only take a single hour. Usually the spirit is happy with the owner gathering non-electronic information for it in return for its ser- vices. KROM Module

name: Infravision Receptors
description: At a cost of one Quintessence, this biomod allows the user to see heat signatures instead of visual light. This allows better vision in the dark, particularly of living creatures. Thus, rolls to see something in the dark are at -3 difficulty, and when trying to see super- naturally hidden objects, the biomod’s Arete is added to the Perception dice pool. However, there is a +3 difficulty to see things that operate at room tempera- ture (such as Vampires). Nanotech Medichines

name: Inhabited Car
description: While Scientists and Technologists is working ex- tremely hard to develop self-driving cars, Dreamspeak- ers have had little difficulty for quite some time. In- stead of developing complex algorithms and control mechanics, they awaken the spirit of the car and per- suade it to help out. When driving itself, the car has a dice pool equal to its Maneuver rating, when being driven by the owner they get a -2 difficulty on all rolls. Iron Kraken

name: Innovation, Inc.'s Personal Lift Generator 5000
description: A motorcycle helmet with a 4 inch helicopter blade mounted on top. It needs to be attached to a rein- forced frame (to protect the user’s neck and spine), but with a handheld control unit providing maneuverabil- ity, it can be used to fly. It has a range of about 50 miles, a maximum height of 5000 feet, and it can trav- el safely up to 40 mph, though it’s max speed is 80 mph. It is often referred to by the tongue-in-cheek name “the 250-hp Beanie.” Juk Ak

name: Invisible Explosive
description: Actually quite visible. However, it leaves no trace behind after the explosion, nothing for forensics to identify at all. While the force can be discovered, there is no particulate matter left behind and whatever is destroyed was not obviously destroyed by explosives. A side effect of this is that no mundane security can de- tect them. Kid Kimota's Jovian Thundergloves

name: Ionic Disruptor
description: The size and shape of a large pistol, the Ionic Dis- rupter ignores Ionic Cloth’s protective properties, Weapons 145 shredding it without effort, bypassing any armor it provides. "Penetrator" Iteration MP-0

name: Iron Kraken
description: A giant metal squid capable of underwater travel. It burns one Quintessence per hour in fuel (Abundanti’s Oil). This is enough for 10 yards/turn of movement, which can be increased to 15 at the cost of one Quin- tessence of fuel per minute. The craft also has a spring- powered screw. One hour of powered movement will wind it up fully, and it can move the craft at 8 yards/turn for a half hour. Some even come equipped with sails for surface travel in case they run out of oil, though they can only manage 4-5 yards/turn. The Iron Kraken can carry 20 armed men and three light cannons (which can be deployed on board). The most dramatic weapons are ten tentacles, each of which has Strength 5 and can be operated by a trained crewman using their own Dexterity. It has an Armor rating of 9. Jonah's Chariot

name: Iron Lung
description: By replacing one of the host's lungs, this device con- verts Carbon Dioxide in the blood into oxygen, which is stored in a compressed form for later use, and car- bon, which can be removed from an opening in the ribcage. Keypads

name: Ixos Acid
description: An extremely powerful acid that uses Entropy 4 to destroy organic objects and even some sturdier ones. One use is all that is required to destroy a letter, but 5 for a body and a whole bookshelf would take 10. Few people use more for a specific task as Ixos Acid is usu- ally considered "sorcerous" within the Order of Rea- son, and so is treated as an illicit substance. It CAN be used as a weapon (doing damage as per Entropy 4 with Arete 4) but doing so uses up the whole flask and al- most certainly causes problems for the user. Slip-Rex, The King of Lubricants

name: Jagg'd Blade of Rending
description: A rather overcomplicated polearm which functions (barely) as a halberd in the hands of a Sleeper, but with +1 difficulty. For the Awakened or at least a Supernat- urally Aware being, the penalty is dropped. But if they know how, they can access a secret catch to activate the device. When activated, the head of the polearm’s many moving parts engage. This improves the balance bringing the difficulty of an attack with the Blade down to 5 and it causes Strength + 7 Aggravated dam- age (the sheer destructive power is due to an Entropy 4 effect). Justice Blades

name: Jangler Pod
description: Triggered by a flare-up in the Ascension War, the Traditions and the Technocracy competing for a sud- den large pool of the newly Awakened, Jangler, a Ver- bena healer, pushed his Technomagickal pod into use before working all the bugs out. As a consequence, it is thoroughly field tested, but the vulgarity of its healing abilities causes it to be unreliable. An Arete roll is made, difficulty 7 (8 with Sleeper witnesses), and on a Life and Nature 75 success it takes an hour to heal the person inside it fully, leaves a few scars, and the patient must rest of several hours. On a failure it causes intense pain, but no injury. A botch, however, causes 5 Aggravated damage to the person inside the pod, and coming from Paradox makes it even deadlier than other things, this is usually enough to kill anyone who is injured enough to be a patient. Each activation costs two Quintessence and requires an Intelligence + Technology roll (difficulty 8). If failed, this increases the difficulty of the Arete roll by 2. Martian Purifier

name: Jonah's Chariot
description: Named somewhat sarcastically, this is not a chariot but a shallow-diving submarine with room for a crew of 10: eight of whom man retractable oars. It travels at 8 meters/turn and, for up to six turns, can increase that to 15 with an engine. There is a ramming spike on the front, and though the Chariot has an Armor rating of 8, only a few damage levels are necessary to cause a leak. Transit and Communication 127 Micron Light Cycle

name: Juk Ak
description: The physical form of the soul of a Wu-Keng. A Juk Ak will be a jade bracelet that can resize itself to fit its wearer. Though it grants no direct control over the Mage it comes from, it is an extremely close Corre- spondence link and generally Mages will go to great lengths to get their souls back. Lon McAin's Cool Shoes

name: Jump Box
description: Like Seekers, these are enhanced software on disks that appear as a small black box with a red button when the Mage enters the Digital Web. There, it can be used to trigger the Instant Offline rote. Net Gear

name: Justice Blades
description: These blades are created to bring a criminal to jus- tice. When created, a specific crime that has gone un- punished is chosen. When used against anyone who did not participate in the crime, the wielder suffers a Weapons 135 +2 difficulty and -5 damage penalty. These are re- versed, however, on those who participated in the crime. Furthermore, the wielder gains the Code of Honor and Iron Will merits for the duration of a sce- ne in which they are fighting one of the perpetrators. Some of these blades have been infused with Quin- tessence, giving them 1-3 attacks that do Aggravated damage. Each of these attacks increases the point cost by 1. Komo-ho'ali'i's Gift

name: Kahu Huruhuru (Feather Cloak)
description: A long cloak made out of feathers. It is beautiful and gives the wearer a regal bearing that is hard to ignore. All social rolls are at -3 difficulty and the wearer gets three dice of countermagick. Kaleidoscopic REM Suit

name: Kaleidoscopic REM Suit
description: These spy suits were designed by Etherites to be used by Sleeper allies, which can not only change their ap- pearance but also emphasizes its effect through pher- omones. The powers are vulgar. On an Arete roll, dif- ficulty 6, it gives the wearer one of the following: Wearables 171 • Camouflage - effective Arcane of 4 • Officer’s Dress - Four dots in Leadership • Mod - Four additional dots of Charisma Robes of Blessing

name: Keypads
description: Microcomputers embedded into the fingers of an operative, they can determine counters to the security measures of any vault, keypad or other security device the agent touches. This reduces the difficulty of Lock- picking, Security and Technology rolls (to break through security) by three. Kinetic Legs

name: Khalil aba-Malek, The Iron Satan
description: Created by the greatest of the Devil-Kings, al-Malek al-Majin ibn Iblis, a scorpion-like machine. Supposed- ly, it slew dozens of mages and hundreds of their fol- lowers before the Devil-king was defeated, after which it burst into flames and disappeared without a trace. The device is possessed, animated and protected by minor demons, and appears to be purely mechanical device. It swings gargantuan swords and stirs up sand in a powerful wind causing any who fight it to add 3 to all difficulties, including magick. It has Speed 8, Armor 7, for weapons it has huge blades (12 dice difficulty 8), sandstorm as above, fire "breath" (8 dice aggravated in 60' stream, difficulty 5, 10 uses), spiked legs (4 dice, difficulty 7) and Strength 10. Lab Assistant

name: Kid Kimota's Jovian Thundergloves
description: Weapons 151 A weapon created for the last days of the Ascension War, several of these gloves exist and tend to fall into the hands of Mages whose sense of self-preservation is somewhat lacking. The gauntlet is made of metal, leather and glass and, once primed, gathers static elec- tricity, as well as electricity from remote Tesla coils, to discharge a powerful bolt of electricity at a target. This is a Forces 3/Prime 2 effect, along with a Correspond- ence 3/Forces 2 effect to draw the electricity. The glove’s insulation is good, but if the character is not grounded, is wet, or otherwise conductive, they take the full damage from the effect. The attack is capable of frying electronics. Without the Tesla coil, the glove gives +3 Bashing damage in hand to hand as a weapon, and can conduct one 6-dice attack on contact. With it, however, the 152 Mystic Armory character can gain 25 quintessence per hour (for the glove) and each blast drains one Quintessence and requires two turns to recharge. This is vulgar. Lash of Passion

name: Kinetic Legs
description: Mechanical internals for legs, replacing fragile bone and muscle. These can multiply the host's kinetic ener- gy, and magickal successes can be applied directly to jumping and running rolls. Lazarus Transmitter

name: Kinetic Transfer Safeguard (KTS)
description: A slightly oversized trench coat full of piezoelectric crystals. It converts kinetic energy into electricity, pro- tects the wearer from the shocks, and electrocutes the next person to touch the activated coat. When hit with an attack, it rolls Arete and reduces the damage taken by the amount that a Forces attack with the Arete roll attained would cause. The next thing touched then takes that damage. Leadstopper Vest

name: Komo-ho'ali'i's Gift
description: Either a club or dagger made with shark-teeth, used to bind a shark spirit into it. Normally, this weapon will deal normal damage. However, it rolls Arete on attacks against supernatural beings that are not spirits, and if it gets two or more successes at difficulty 6, the damage is doubled. This does not use Quintessence, however, if the Quintessence ever runs out, the spirit is released and the item becomes an ordinary knife or club. Magick Sword Coin

name: KROM Module
description: a By taking a fungus that changes color when exposed to a laser and confining it to a glass case, Virtual Adepts can use a Kaleidoscopic Repigmentation Or- ganic Memory Module to store massive amounts of data in a very small area (a 75-mm square will store 25.5 TB). Any data stored is lost if the fungus is ex- posed to white light. Magickal Macro Keyboard

name: Lab Assistant
description: A small robot on dreads with a small frame, telescop- ing arm and articulated digits for grasping and a cam- era and light for vision. It also has a pair of VR goggles that, when worn, allow the owner to see and hear what the robot does, and manipulate its digits at a distance. Other Beings 103 Medi-Bot

name: Lady Wudlowe's Menhirs
description: 124 Mystic Armory A menhir, or giant stone monument, that contains a hidden compartment that the Lady Wudlowe of the Verbena used to help her escape Four of them have survived, even though six were made. They stand eight feet tall and weigh nearly a ton each, so they are rarely moved. Each has a Celtic cross on it which, when touched, draws aside the front to allow entry. Once inside, the user teleports to the location foremost in their mind. Unfortunately, these were made before OSHA com- pliance, and neither Lady Wudlowe nor Nemue (who crafted them with her) thought to include an interior trigger. It was discovered centuries later that one of them had run out of power with Lady Wudlowe trapped inside. Matter Transmission Portal

name: Lash of Passion
description: Normally, just a last that does Strength + 1 damage with a difficulty of 7, and can be used to grab objects or the grapple with difficulty 8, it also has the follow- ing powers, rolled with Arete: • (Mind 1) Grasping the handle causes the holder to be filled with self-confidence and a sense of superi- ority • (Mind 2) Though damage and pain occur normally, those hit with the whip will also find it pleasurable. Repeated use will train the target that pain is pleas- ure and that inflicting pain on others is fun for eve- ryone. This effect lasts for the duration of the ef- fect, and can be resisted with Willpower • (Entropy 3) The last can slice through solid materi- als (as Destroy Matter). This is vulgar. • (Entropy 4) By using a command word in a foul language during an attack, the wounds caused by the last will fester immediately, and this spreads if not magickally healed. The victim loses one Health Level per day unless healed. Furthermore, with three successes on the attack roll, a limb on the tar- get is disabled. This is vulgar. Limitless Bow

name: Lawai'a Tatu
description: Typically only given to great fisherman, the Lawai’a Tatu consists of three wavy lines on either side of the neck and vaguely resembles gills. Whenever the wear- er’s head goes below water, the tatu becomes gills, al- lowing them to breathe. Nine-Dragon Tattoos

name: Lazarus Transmitter
description: This cybernetic implant created by the Technocracy sits inside the skull and hooks into the brain. If the Mage is ever mortally injured, the Mage is projected fully into the Digital Web: physically, mentally and spiritually, where they can stay until their body is re- paired. Mapping Implant

name: Leadstopper Vest
description: With heavy treatment, any clothing can be made stronger. This particular treatment is so sturdy that it Wearables 167 gives four dice of armor, and without any penalties, so that many Syndicate agents who can afford it have everything they wear regularly treated to become Leadstopper Vest. Martinez Robust Hardsuit

name: Lemegeton, The
description: Also called the Lesser Key of Solomon (though few seriously believe it to have been written by the man), the Lemegeton is a very detailed descriptions of Angels and Demons, including rites for summoning them and what correspondences need to be understood. The Lemegeton acts as the Library background up to level 2-4, and can teach the Cosmology, Enigmas, Lore (spirits), Metaphysics and Occult Knowledges. The Liber Labyrinthus

name: Leng Chao's Chamber of Yin-Yang
description: Disappointed in exercise equipment as both tying him down and failing to challenge him, the Akashic Leng Chao built a unique training chamber. It consists of a spherical chamber, four meters in diameter that spins on a platform. It also contains a system of anti- gravity fields to give the user control over the strength of gravity within the chamber. The low-gravity allowed him to focus on Dexterity and balance, the high-gravity added resistance, forcing his muscles to work harder than they ever would in normal gravity. 16 Mystic Armory Any exercise sequence starts with an Arete roll to set the gravity and to put it into motion. The Chamber will continue as long as the occupant does, drawing Quintessence from the user and, if they pass the point of exhaustion they can roll Willpower (difficulty 8), and the Quintessence will be fed back. This whole process takes 10 minutes per point of Stamina, five minutes to draw one point of Quintes- sence and five minutes to return it. If the Mage does not complete the whole time, their Quintessence stays in the device and the Mage is exhausted. If they do make it through, then they are energized and alert. If the occupant is inside beyond this, however, it be- gins to drain Quintessence at twice the normal rate, the Willpower roll increases to difficulty 9 at the end of this phase, and each time that they continue at the end of a phase, the drain speed doubles again and the Willpower difficulty increases by one. The Mage will survive this for one sequence per point of Stamina. If they go beyond this, they will die, leaving their Quintessence filling the Chamber. The Lens of Zadkiel

name: Lens of Zadkiel, The
description: Zadkiel is an angel of knowledge and understanding, so anyone who looks through the six-inch crystal lens that it is bound into (with Enochian written around the circumference) will see everything translated for them, from languages to high-level encryption and even werewolf glyphs and other strange signs. The lens can also see through illusions of all sorts. If being used against an active power, a Gnosis roll is necessary against it. PDNA

name: Lethe's Spheres
description: Alchemical glass spheres containing the same vapors as the Vap'rous Candles of Lethe. When thrown and shattered, these spheres have the same effect as the candles: they put people in the room to sleep after Stamina + 1 turns. Nightmare Field Generator

name: Liber Labyrinthus, The
description: The Book of the Labyrinth is viewed widely as ob- scene, and contains huge amounts of information on the Wyrm and its servants, including (but not limited to) the names, correspondences and rites of the Urge- spirits. Can act as the Library background up to level 5, and can teach the Cosmology, Enigmas, Lore (spirits), Met- aphysics and Occult Knowledges. The Liber Spiritum

name: Liber Spiritum, The
description: Not truly a specific book, but rather a class of them, a Book of Spirt is created by most Mages who deal with extra-worldly beings. The most common organizational scheme is that every left-hand page gives the name and seal of a spirit, and the right-hand gives the summon- ing ritual. A summoned spirit marks the book and can be called again whenever the reader wishes. Can act as the Library background up to level 1-5, and can teach the Cosmology, Enigmas, Lore (spirits), Metaphysics and Occult Knowledges. Liber Tenebris Distalis

name: Liber Tenebris Distalis
description: A Nephandic grimoire that Hermetics can (at great risk) glean some insight from, this tome covers deep demonology and foul rituals. It contains True Names for mid-level Banes and demons, and can be used to learn Arete 3 and Spirit 3. However, learning from this tome puts a non-Nephandus at risk: any time they perform magick using Spirit, a botch will cause their Avatar to invert and they become Widderslainte. procedure.rand.enlight/40.4292.18.23.c

name: Light Meter
description: Adjustments in lighting can alter a scene, mostly in subtle ways. Some examples of how lighting can change a situation are: • Increase color temperature - Creates a sterile envi- ronment making rolls relating to sensing emotions harder, with the Agent’s Arete rolled to remove successes • Decrease color temperature - Creates a sense of nostalgia, similar to the Sugar Magnolias rote, with each success increasing dice pools for remembering past events • Extremely high-key lighting - this sort of intense lighting is useful for interrogations, and anyone subject to it for an hour must succeed at a Will- power roll or else lose a point of Willpower. Mad Fiddles of Dr. Mercer

name: Lighter-Than-Air Masticated Conveyance Gel
description: Spratt and Dabbet (a novelty company run by a pair of Etherites) has developed a pink or green lump of soft putty that, when chewed, produces a gas that is so much lighter than air that a bubble blown in it will be able to lift the chewer off the ground. If they have not made prior plans, they are at the mercy of winds. For- tunately, though, these bubbles are stronger than those of bubblegum and will only pop if aggressively stabbed with a sharp object. Siren's Tears and the Breather Collar

name: Limitless Bow
description: Once an array if fired from this bow, arrows will ma- terialize, ready to fire until either the magic dissipates or an arrow is removed from the bow, unfired. Lustral Water

name: Link Collar
description: All the advantages of mundane mirrorshades for hid- ing the eyes of the wearer, they can also spend Quin- tessence to, one-for-one, remove successes from mind- influencing effects targeting the wearer. A single point can negate any nonmagickal roll to detect the wearer’s lies or motives. Personality Software 90 Mystic Armory

name: Lon McAin's Cool Shoes
description: These shoes are designed to ensure that Void Engi- neers are not lost when outside a ship doing repairs, or even just dangling by a tether at high speeds. They add four dice to Dexterity + Athletics pools that would benefit from the special soles that allow the shoes to stick to any surface. Mask of Silent Death

name: Looking Glass
description: A small, plain, brass make-up compact. Aside from its mundane use, it can also be used to scry any loca- tion within a mile radius by looking into the internal mirror. Lycanthroscope

name: Lucky Coin
description: A shiny coin carved with mandalas. It was created by a con artist and it shows: the coin grants good luck in gambling and helps when trying to talk someone out of their money, whether by running a con or applying for a job. Rolling the coin's Arete allows the owner to increase their odds when gambling of success by up to a factor of 10 for each success on the roll. When not gambling but otherwise trying to make money, it de- creases difficulty on Leadership, Performance or Sub- terfuge by 3. Small coincidences will also guarantee that the owner of this coin always has Resources 2. Parking Karma

name: Lustral Water
description: Supposedly the tears or sweat of the gods, Lustral Water removes the ability of those it is poured on to do magick, temporarily. It must be thrown from within 10 meters of the target and a hit must be scored, then six dice are rolled (difficulty 9). The number of suc- cesses, plus three, is subtracted from the target’s Arete, Blood Pool, Gnosis or any other magickal ability or power. This lasts for one turn per point of Willpower of the user, and there is no known way to resist its effects. Mask of the Warrior

name: Lycanthroscope
description: A fairly common looking meter-reader, the meter on the box shows higher numbers when pointing in the direction of a werewolf. A needle indicates intensity and a light flashes when the scientist using it gets too close to a werewolf. Variants can be found that detect vampires, wraiths, the fae and other sorts of supernat- ural creature. This device grants the user +2 dice on any Percep- tion + Awareness roll to detect a werewolf. Magnifying Glass

name: Mad Fiddles of Dr. Mercer
description: Violins created in the early years of the 20th Century, they’re modified to have small, tin microamps inside that can alter the subharmonics of the violin. This allows it to project an effect that drives the passions of the audience wild. Its Arete is rolled, and everyone has to roll Willpower (difficulty 7, +1 for every two suc- cesses on a Manipulation + Expression roll from the fiddler). Madness Grenade

name: Mad Mook's Million Eyes on the World
description: Rings and necklaces that look like toy jewelry with an eye theme. Created by a Marauder, they cause the wearer to feel a slight buzzing in the back of their head and their vision becomes fuzzy. The piece of jewelry rolls Arete when put on to determine if the effect oc- curs. If it does, it stops when the item is removed, though it is likely to linger for a time afterwards. The main effect is to give the wearer Matter, Mind, Life, Prime, Spirit and Time perceptions simultaneous- ly, not simplifying so that they can be parsed separate- ly. This causes a +5 difficulty to all Perception difficul- ties, and the world they perceive is weird enough that they start losing track of what is and isn’t real. Next, the wearer starts to hear the surface thoughts of people around her, particularly negative ones and even more negative thoughts about the wearer. This creates a feedback loop between odd behavior of the wearer and negative thoughts towards them. Thoughts and The ability of powerful Mind mages to manipulate the thoughts and memories of others is one of the most feared powers in their arsenal. Worse, when they make Wonders, these powers become available to oth- ers who wouldn’t be able to do it otherwise, often without having to work very hard at all. Cephalic VCR

name: Madam Xanadu's All-Seeing Fortune Machine
description: For the cost of a coin and an Arete roll (difficulty 6), a Mage can use this clockwork fortune-teller to get short, enigmatic bit of advice. It asks the Mage to think deeply on its problem, and then a 10-word phrase will be printed on a ticket and come out through the slot. The advice is sometimes specific, though, having given names, addresses, telephone numbers, and even occa- sionally money, to desperate querents. Pele's Lamaku

name: Madness Grenade
description: A glass sphere filled with insanity spirits, it explodes, releasing them, when thrown. The result is described as a “screaming black-light rainbow-storm.” The effect of throwing on is an Entropy 5/Mind 4/Matter 3 ex- plosion of madness and decay using the spirits as a focus, for a radius of 20 yards around the center of the blast. It also produces a Mind 4 effect causing insanity, an Entropy 5 effect destroying order, and an Entropy 3/Matter 3 effect that breaks things. Fortunately, this is vulgar on Earth, though it is quite coincidental in the Umbra, and likely to attract more spirits to join the fun. Oriole of Tranquility

name: Magick Sword Coin
description: A coin attached to the hilt of a sword, this Talisman grants Spatial Perceptions to the user, allowing them to fight without the benefit of their usual senses. Fur- thermore, it can use Forces 2 to blind an opponent with a bright flash of light. The light beam is vulgar, and requires a point of quintessence for each turn it is in use. Organic Knife

name: Magnatronic III VASP Computer
description: Voice activated with output sent to a pair of glasses (often mirrorshades), the VASP is a Trinary computer the size of a pager and with the ability to wirelessly access nonencrypted data on other nearby machines. A new user needs to spend about three hours training it to recognize their voice, and Sleepers who attempt to use it fry it unless the VASD succeeds in an Arete roll at difficulty 8. Mark IV Hand Computer

name: Magnifying Glass
description: When used to search a crime scene, a Magnifying Glass helps find and explain clues. A single success on a Perception + Investigation roll reveals a clue, three gleans a fact from the clue and five pieces together some aspect of the criminal's appearance. Each roll costs one Quintessence and it can only be used once per location. Manar Scanner

name: Manar Scanner
description: On the outside, it’s a radar scanner. On the inside, though, it’s much more complex and instead locates concentrations of Quintessence characteristic of mag- ick, picking out Mages and Wonders. For an extra cost of two background points, it can even determine if a person is possessed or other similar influences. The Mirror of Penthesilea

name: Mapping Implant
description: This implant contains a GPS unit as well as a data- base of maps covering the world, particularly with road maps and detailed maps of major cities. More maps can be uploaded through a port behind the user’s ear. It can overlay one of these maps on the user’s vision so that they can see exactly where they are. Multi-Purpose Computer Implant

name: Mark IV Hand Computer
description: Originally designed as a palmtop computer, they now more closely resemble smartphones and can be mistaken for one easily by the untrained eye. More powerful than the average laptop, the Mark IV is com- patible with all Iteration X technology, including Digi- tal Web interfaces. Master Remote

name: Mark VII Cassini AUC
description: At roughly 100 meters long, the Cassini Armored Universal Cruiser can handle a total of 80 crew and passengers. However, it only requires a five person crew to operate under ideal conditions. Designed to explore the Deep Universe, the Cassini has four AFC arrays (four guns each), heavy duty energy shielding, and three STAR units. QUEST Transport

name: Martian Purifier
description: 76 Mystic Armory A pen shaped device supposedly based on ancient Martian technology, it will work ten times before it wears out (tracked with Quintessence that cannot be replaced). It and clear the various pathways of a human body, dealing with depression, fatigue, minor aches and pains and simple illnesses. Med Pack

name: Martinez Robust Hardsuit
description: A bulky suit that almost qualifies as a small mecha: seven and a half feet tall, not even remotely conceala- ble, and designed for the harshest environments and missions. A full five dice of armor supported by servos giving +3 Strength, though unable to run or jump, only walk and jog. The internal climate control can handle -40 to 140 degrees Fahrenheit. The gloves of the suit are reinforced to cause an additional die of damage, and optionally can deploy blades to convert damage to Lethal. All of these require a total of one point of Quintessence per hour of operation. 168 Mystic Armory If the suit is penetrated and the wearer takes damage, an Arete roll is necessary. On a botch, the suit breaks a down, disabling all powers and modules. The suit can have up to five Hardsuit Modules add- ed to it. Power Suit

name: Iago's Mask
description: a A Greek funeral mask with a joyless smile made of weathered bronze with a black silk cord. It conceals the wearer’s identity and causes arcane connections (other than to a Talisman or Familiar) to the wearer to fail. Even the wearer’s Resonance and Synergy are hidden from detection. These effects work retroactively, so long as the mask is worn, though they can be turned off for a turn through a mental command. It is gener- ally worn by the leader of the Golden Chalice sect of the Euthanatoi. Space Jam

name: Mask Maker, The
description: Though Progenitors change their faces and bodies for disguises, that carries risks and is not particularly popular among Iterators. Instead, they use Mask Mak- ers. These devices have two components: a microfabri- cation unit capable of creating believable masks, finger print duplicators and contact lenses to reproduce reti- nal patterns, and a sampling device that is used to input the pattern to create. For facial and voice dis- guises, only 10 minutes of video of the target are nec- essary, and only 20 minutes are needed to create the mask. Fingerprints and retinal patterns require some means of obtaining them from the target. The device and produce 12 masks before refilling, but the materials it uses are expensive and require Resources 3 to acquire, or appropriate Requisitions. PlastiSkin

name: Mask of Silent Death
description: Created by Euthanatoi to aid in their work, these masks decrease the difficulty of stealth rolls by 3 when worn. Further, the mask's Arete can be rolled at diffi- culty 4 to decrease the difficulty on a damage roll by one per success. The Masquer's Grand Disguise

name: Mask of the Warrior
description: A mask containing a token from a dead warrior, the Mask of the Warrior is a relatively common fetish. Usually carved from wood and with a piece of a great warrior’s weapon inside (among traditional Dreamspeakers, more urban ones will use more mod- ern things like ski-masks and military uniforms) these masks add three dots to the wearer’s Melee, two dots to Athletics for the purpose of dodging attacks, and one dot to Brawl, all of them up to a maximum of six. Some add to Firearms instead of Melee. In all cases, the mask gives the user the Short Fuse flaw. Some of the more powerful masks, connected to the spirits of particularly great and dangerous warriors, also provide the Berserker merit. These are the higher cost variant. Mu-Jen

name: Master Joro's Sash
description: Life and Nature 77 Made by a 16th century Japanese Akashic, this while silk sash absorbs damage for the wearer. It can absorb one point of damage for each point of the wearer’s Arete and turns from white to pink to red as it takes the damage. Each health level absorbed costs one Quintessence. This is vulgar. The sash can be lightened (releasing on point of damage) for each hour the wear- er spends meditating while wearing it. Medicine Bag

name: Master Remote
description: This remote can control any piece of technology that has a means of information transfer that would allow any hypothetical remote to work on it. The lettering on top changes during use to show the available op- tions on the technology being controlled. MEGA Pen

name: Medi-Bot
description: A small, disk-shaped robot that stands six inches high and is 18 inches in diameter. With four metallic limbs that can hold medical tools, it can perform most surgeries, deliver anesthetics, and stabilize patients. It has an effective Intelligence of 4 and Medicine of 4, and by spending one point of Quintessence per pa- tient, it can treat multiple patients at once. This cost is per patient and per hour. Verrecchia's Marvelous Lions

name: Medicine Bag
description: Unrelated to the medicine pouches of the Americas, an African medicine bag holds bones, shells, twisted roots, sticks and teeth for use in summoning spirits. The spirits that are summoned then focus on healing. In fact, any Life or Spirit focused effect whose intent is to heal where the medicine bag is used as an instru- ment gains one die. Tonics and Potions

name: MEGA Pen
description: Containing both a HEAT processor and a KROMM unit, McPherson’s Eternal Greatness Assured (MEGA) Pen is a huge step forward in miniaturized computing. The pen tip doesn't write, instead it is a laser projector that can create a computer display on any flat surface, removing the need for a monitor or other screen. Un- fortunately, it has been miniaturized to the point that everything done with this unique Device is vulgar. Mental Enhancement Glasses

name: Michael Durmstrang's Creepy-Ass China Doll
description: An empty-eyed china doll created to store Quintes- sence for use in necromantic rituals. Mokomai

name: Micro Tool Kit
description: Engineering and Materials 37 A pouch the size of a hard sunglasses case filled with tools. These are virtually standard equipment within the Technocratic Union, as they always have the right tool for the job at hand within them. They come spe- cialized for an Ability, most commonly Technology, Medicine or Investigation, though Computer, Survival and Expression (Disguise) are common as well. The use of an appropriate toolkit gives -3 difficulty to rolls on that ability. The Quintessence stored in the Tool Kit can be used to recharge almost any Technocratic Device. It also comes in the form of gloves with tools em- bedded in the fingertips, but this is an older, less popu- lar design. Perfected Focus

name: Micro-RPV
description: Drones the size of a coin, these are Iteration X’s best surveillance systems. They can travel at 15 mph and transmit and receive signals from up to a mile away. On top of the small size, they are also designed to ap- pear as insects, further helping them blend in when surveilling a target. Chargeable with regular electricity, they can only fly for one hour or transmit for a day before a recharge. Node Seekers Technomancer’s Toybox page 51(as Seekers) Genetically engineered cockroaches, Seekers were created by the Progenitors to hunt down hidden Nodes and stores of Tass. With a detection range of 10 miles, an Arete roll is made and the roaches scatter on a failure, but home in on the source if successful with difficulty determined by strength of the source: Source Difficulty Huge Node (Level 5+/50 Tass) 6 Large Node (Level 4/40 Tass) 7 Strong Node (Level 3/30 Tass) 8 Weak Node (Level 2/20 Tass) 9 Tiny Node (Level 1/10 Tass) 10 If a person is caught in a swarm of Seekers, they are subject to the Seeker Plague. They must make a Stami- na roll at difficulty 8 to avoid contracting it, and if they get it, it is extremely unlikely to be cured without mag- ick of some sort. Another Stamina roll determines the severity: success gives a 48-hour recovery time, but failure can be disabling for months and a botch indi- cates death in hours. Panoramic Surveillance Node

name: Micron Light Cycle
description: A sleek Japanese motorcycle, very popular among individual Union Agents. With no particular weapons or armor, it really only has one merit: it is fast! Its top speed is 150 miles per hour and has a range of about 500 miles. Further, it can easily weave in and out of traffic, it has a built-in communicator and tracking devices, and a storage compartment. Multi-Terrain Explorer

name: MIDAS Card
description: 30 Mystic Armory

name: Mirror of Penthesilea, The
description: A small, wooden hand mirror, the Mirror of Penthe- silea shows the inner state of a woman looking in it, regardless of how they look normally. Inner beauty and wickedness are the thing that shine through. Orbital Manar Station

name: Mirrorshades
description: All the advantages of mundane mirrorshades for hid- ing the eyes of the wearer, they can also spend Quin- tessence to, one-for-one, remove successes from mind- influencing effects targeting the wearer. A single point can negate any nonmagickal roll to detect the wearer’s lies or motives. Personality Software 90 Mystic Armory

name: Mjollnir Handguns
description: Void Engineer heavy weaponry, an a terrible idea to use on Earth. It fires a bolt of plasma that will inciner- ate virtually everything. It inflicts Aggravated Forces damage, and requires the Energy Weapons skill to fire. Furthermore, it’s heavy enough that anyone with Strength less than 3 suffers a one die penalty per dot less. P22 Gauss Needler

name: Mobile Home
description: A silver holder the size and weight of a cigarette case which folds out to form a door-size metal frame. When it is stood upright, it becomes a doorway to a Horizon Realm, spending one point of Quintessence for each person who passes through it in either direction. It is sophisticated enough that the owner can mentally specify an authorized user list. If an unauthorized user tries to use it, they will take three levels of Aggravated damage from an electrical shock which uses up a point of Quintessence. Finally, the owner may, so long as the Unique and The Orb of Honorius 98 Mystic Armory Mobile Home is in their possession, meditate any- where and regain Quintessence from a Node within the Horizon Realm it is keyed to. a Omnichronal Watch

name: Mokomai
description: There is a belief that Quintessence (mana) pools in the head. This has led some Polynesian Mages to create periapts from human heads. The heads must be intact and either on a special rack or tied together via their hair. The Mokomai regains one Quintessence per week naturally, and the Quintessence it holds is accessible to the Mage. If it is ever drained completely, it must be re- enchanted. A normal human head will hold one point, the head of a kahuna (Mage) or powerful warrior will hold 2, and a proper chieftain or a Rokea (or other shapeshifter) can hold 3. Sea Spirit Shell

name: Molecular Phone
description: A phone small enough to be built into mirrorshades or other articles of clothing, it is much smaller than a cellular phone, hence the name. Functionally a cellular phone, but with better reception and better voice command features. Tsu-Ti (Divine Bamboo)

name: Mu-Jen
description: Usually made from special wood and carved with a consecrated silver blade, the Mu-Jen is a doll that re- sembles the victim. When complete, the creator rolls Willpower. If successful, it is activated and no one else can use it. By spending 4 of the Mu-Jen’s Quintessence and then damaging it, the damage is inflicted upon the target as Aggravated damage. A warning for would-be creators: any botch on the creation or the Willpower roll results in a Mu-Jen targeting the creator. Nine Jade Dragons

name: Multi-Purpose Computer Implant
description: An advanced implant computer that is not quite as sophisticated as an Digital Enhancement Implants, but is just short of it, the MPCI can access the user’s eyes and ears and acts as an equivalent of the Digital Inter- face Armband. Further, it gives the user both the Lightning Calculator and the Time Sense merits. The computer included is equivalent to the Mark IV Hand Computer and has a concealed port behind the user’s right ear. Given the access to both eyes and ears, the costs of the Mapping Implant and the Hidioshi Wearable Translator are both reduced by one point for the user. With an Implant Radio, the user can access it like a wireless modem and can connect wirelessly to devices as well as to the Internet and Digital Web. Night Eyes

name: Multi-Terrain Explorer
description: A hovercraft designed for recon and mapping, the 128 Mystic Armory MTE has no trouble with anything short of dense for- ests and steep mountains. It has a forward mounted chain gun and light armor. The sealed body can hold a pilot, a passenger and some cargo fairly comfortably. It is not really a combat vehicle, but when in combat its chief merit is speed: it can move up to 200 mph and has a range of 1500 miles. Nondescript Van

name: Nanopatch
description: A nanopatch is roughly the size of a nicotine patch and used for emergency first aid. It contains nanites which are applied to the patched area and do some quick-and-dirty repair work, healing a single health level (if Aggravated, the subject must also spend a point of Willpower). Nanovaccine

name: Nanotech Medichines
description: Special medical nanites that are injected into the bloodstream of someone who is injured. These boost healing, and only for the pre-determined individual they’ve been engineered for. Anyone with these nanites rolls Arete (difficulty 6) whenever they are Internal 55 injured. If successful, lethal and Bashing damage is healed at five times the usual rate, whereas failure causes the nanites to fail to register the wound and ignore it. At the cost of a point of Quintessence, the owner may try to force the nanites to recognize wound, getting an additional roll. Physical Structure Enhancement

name: Nanovaccine
description: Though most Progenitor field agents are vaccinated against common illnesses, sometimes they run into something rare or novel. The nanovaccine is a quick injection that will attack any disease that hasn’t reached an irreversibly lethal state, curing it within hours. Mystic Medical science doesn’t have a monopoly on heal- ing. Mystical methods of recovering from illness and injury, or even deflecting it as it happens, have been in use for millennia. Clay Man Amulet

name: Net Gear
description: Less efficient than Virtual Adept models, Iteration X Digital Web interfaces are full suits with gloves, boots, belt and helmet all wired together. They allow sensory immersion into the Digital Web along with creating multiple icons in the Web. Furthermore, it allows a Perception + Investigation roll to locate known places or track other users. Physical Transfer Unit or Suit

name: Night Eyes
description: This gives a human being the same level of night vi- sion that a cat has. Online Access

name: Nightmare Field Generator
description: The size and shape of a desk globe, the Nightmare Field Generator costs one Quintessence per turn to activate. When activated, it searches the minds of eve- ryone who can see it (except those whitelisted in the software) and alters their perceptions of the environ- ment to match their worst fears and nightmares. Sleepwalker's Drum

name: Nine Jade Dragons
description: A six foot shaft topped with a trident and with two crossbars at right angles to each other, the Nine Drag- on Trident is an awkward weapon in the hands of anyone not specifically trained to use it. However, the Nine Jade Dragons is a Nine Dragon Trident made of pure jade and carved with dragons along the shaft. The jade in it is tougher than steel, and in fact it is nigh unbreakable. It can be used even by someone without specialized training, unlike more common Nine Drag- on Tridents, adding one dot of Melee for attacks and two dots for parrying. It does Strength + 5 damage and has Conceal N. Nuclear Bomb

name: Nine-Dragon Tattoos
description: This mystical tattoo was created by the Akashayana, and consists of nine dragons: three red, three green and three white. When in use, the tattoo is not static, the dragons move around the Akashic's body as fol- lows: • The red dragons can detect living things, and when used move to the Akashic's arms and face. The dragons on the arms will roar and flick their tongues in the direction of the sensed life forms. Generally, the dragon on the right arm responds to the physical world and left to the Umbra, and if the lifeform is in the physical world, the wings are tight against the body, but they are unfurled if the lifeform is in the Umbra. • The green dragons reduce entropy. One spreads its wings and dominates the Akashic's back, one the chest, and the third moves around the body and spits fire at sources of Entropy. If the Akashic stretched their arms towards a source of Entropy, this dragon will increase the amount of order pre- sent. • The white dragons can open a gateway into the Umbra. The three move to the Akashic's chest and intertwine their tails and extend their claws. Once they are like this, the Akashic can use them to shred the Gauntlet and create a gap large enough for them to step through. 'O'ole Tatu

name: Node Seekers
description: A marble sized sphere covered in adhesive, the Pano- ramic Surveillance Node has become a staple of the New World Order. When activated, it operates for an hour and can operate for an additional half hour at the cost of a point of Quintessence. It requires no light, but takes a 3D panoramic recording of every- thing happening within 10 yards. This recording co- vers audio, video, dimensional coordinates, and all the Information 43 information necessary to establish a rank 2 Data con- 44 Mystic Armory nection to anything in the recording. Remote Piloted Vehicle

name: Non-Puncturing Injector
description: While Sleepers are still experimenting with air- pressure delivery systems for medicines, the Progeni- tors have already moved beyond it. Using Dimensional Science, the Non-Puncturing Injector can not only inject medicine into the bloodstream without punctur- ing the skin, but can even do so at a distance with a Dexterity + Energy Weapons roll. Each use requires a point of Quintessence. Syringe Pharmacopeia Technocracy: Progenitors pages 48 Appearing to be a syringe, it actually converts stored nonpoisonous or medicinal liquids (usually water) into poisons or sedatives that require a Stamina roll (diffi- culty 9) to resist. Healing A Healing Figurine is a figurine of an animal or a person with a soothing or otherwise benevolent na- ture. When stored in the room of a sick person, it doubles the rate of healing for anyone who rests in that room. Herbal Plaster of the Ancients

name: Nondescript Van
description: The Nondescript Van not only has a paint job that causes it to blend in and stay unnoticed wherever it needs to be, but is designed as a mobile base of opera- tions for any field Amalgam. The walls are laced with Primium for shielding, the inside is collocated to an- other dimension that is sealed from all other entrance or exit, and only accessible by those authorized by the system software: it’s just an empty van to anyone else. Its Arete is rolled to resist any magickal attempt to notice that it is anything but a regular van. Paladin Sedan

name: Nothing to See Here
description: Metal stakes with yellow police tape used to create a barrier by the New World Order when they want peo- ple to ignore some area where non-standard things are happening. It makes the area inside look like a mun- dane crime scene and encourages people to wander off without looking too carefully. Whispering Stone

name: NSN Plasma Caster
description: Void Engineer heavy weaponry, an a terrible idea to use on Earth. It fires a bolt of plasma that will inciner- ate virtually everything. It inflicts Aggravated Forces damage, and requires the Energy Weapons skill to fire. Furthermore, it’s heavy enough that anyone with Strength less than 3 suffers a one die penalty per dot less. P22 Gauss Needler

name: Nuclear Bomb
description: As a gadget, a nuclear bomb can be used by Sleepers suitably trained in their use. This weapon is capable of Weapons 153 destroying an entire city, and at minimum causes 10 levels of unsoakable Aggravated damage, but actual assessments of how much it does are the realm of sto- ry, not dice. Only the most powerful of vulgar magicks can cause someone to survive if they are caught in the blast area. If a character has one and there’s any sign they might use it, then the government or the Union will descend on them to make sure the device is taken out of their hands and they are locked away from long, long time. 154 Mystic Armory Pluvius' Javelinman

name: Oho-Kui (Battle Wig)
description: An enormous headdress of pounded leaves that is worn into battle. It looks somewhat like a bushy, straight-hair wig and it can be dyed any color, though bright colors are preferred. The wearer gains three Bruised Health Levels and three automatic successes on all soak rolls while wearing it. These Health Levels are tied to the Oho-Kui and regenerate at a rate of one per day when not in use. Samson's Gauntlets

name: Omnichronal Watch
description: This watch seems to choose owners: it may appear for an anarchist or someone otherwise on the fringes of society, and it disappears if the owner ever gets a normal job or otherwise turns their back on those principles. The watch can roll Arete at difficulty 6 and every two successes give the wearer an extra action, with the effect lasting for a scene. It also completely stops the aging of the owner for as long as they own a the watch. The most powerful effect is that it allows the user to Sidestep Time for a scene (from their per- spective), and this doesn't require an Arete roll, only the expenditure of three points of Quintessence. The watch disappears if the owner ever works a steady job for more than a day, if they ever have resources higher than three, or if they ever refuse to help someone in need when they have the means to do so. Theresita's Thousand Magick-Finger Bed

name: Online Access
description: A cybernetic enhancement that allows the character to act as though they have an internal smartphone and Internal 65 wireless connection. They can access the internet, GPS etc. This allows sensory access to the Digital Web and most versions include two USB ports. Of course, the internet or Digital Web must be accessible for this to function, so it is substantially less useful in the Umbra. Performance Blade

name: Oracle: The Essence
description: Rumored to be written by Porthos Fitz-Empress, like- ly the last thing he wrote before he died, Oracle is an esoteric role-playing game. Weighing in at about 700 pages of complex rules and bizarre concepts, very few people can understand it well enough to play it. Those who work through it and understand it find them- selves moving through a parable of a Hermetic Awak- ening, and those who truly understand it, enact the rituals inside of it, and works hard have a better than normal chance to Awaken, particularly as a Hermetic. Other Books are not the only tool for learning. Mages have developed numerous other techniques and methods of storing and transmitting their knowledge. Bird Staff of Osanyin

name: Orb of Honorius, The
description: This watch seems to choose owners: it may appear for an anarchist or someone otherwise on the fringes of society, and it disappears if the owner ever gets a normal job or otherwise turns their back on those principles. The watch can roll Arete at difficulty 6 and every two successes give the wearer an extra action, with the effect lasting for a scene. It also completely stops the aging of the owner for as long as they own a the watch. The most powerful effect is that it allows the user to Sidestep Time for a scene (from their per- spective), and this doesn't require an Arete roll, only the expenditure of three points of Quintessence. The watch disappears if the owner ever works a steady job for more than a day, if they ever have resources higher than three, or if they ever refuse to help someone in need when they have the means to do so. Theresita's Thousand Magick-Finger Bed

name: Orbital Manar Station
description: Capable of detecting magick in the greater metropol- itan area of a city, the Orbital Manar Station is unlike- ly to directly be owned by any individual character, though requests can be made of it. Portable Manar Information 49

name: Organic Knife
description: Not actually organic, it's really made of a special sort of plastic. This knife, though, has several properties that take it above and beyond normal plastic knives. When heated to the boiling point of water (by submer- 136 Mystic Armory sion in water or by 30 seconds in the microwave) it becomes very pliable and can be molded into a variety of shapes that are quite unassuming. Once cool, it is extremely hard and scratch resistant, but otherwise just normal plastic. When struck hard against a hard sur- face, the knife returns to its initial shape Primium Blade

name: Orrery of Madame des Bellestours
description: An Orrery created by Madame des Bellestours, the very existence of this model of the Solar System is evi- dence of the fact that she was a hero of the Enlight- enment. It is an accurate clockwork model, with many moons of Jupiter and Saturn that were not known to scientists of the time. Some theorize that it is tied more closely to the planets and moons than it should be and some Correspondence and Dimensional Science based studies suggest that this may be the case, but certainly no one has tested it, and few believe it is anything of significance. Soul Mates

name: P22 Gauss Needler
description: This pistol fires metallic needles at high velocity. Enough that it will shred flesh but can’t puncture the hull of a ship, making it ideal for space combat. It hits like a normal Firearm, and does damage as a Mat- ter/Forces effect. PRETI gun

name: Pahu Ino-Nui
description: Life and Nature 79 An enormous, six-foot-long by five-foot diameter drum, only two Chaos Drums are believed to have ever been made. The user beats the drum in time with the weather, and after fifteen minutes, the user can change their pattern and the weather will change with it. Play- ing the drum is exhausting, and a character with Stam- ina 2 will only manage five minutes. Any Mage without Stamina 5 should use Life magick to increase their stamina before attempting to use the Chaos Drum. The Drum itself maintains a circle of about ten feet of absolute calm, even when the Drum is causing ty- phoons, tsunamis and volcanic eruptions. Seeds of Decay

name: Paladin Sedan
description: As the Sprectre Limousines are often too recogniza- ble, the Paladin Sedan has been developed. Usually a dark blue or silver sedan or mid-size car, they can get any modification available on a Spectre. Further, it is a fully electrical vehicle, running on the Quintessence stored within its battery. Rumor has it that a few SUVs have been built to Paladin specs, though if they exist they are quite hard to obtain. Rocket Chariot

name: Paradox Stone
description: numbers) In the past, these charms were made in decently large numbers, but they're hard to make and anyone who gets one is likely to hoard it. Often taking the form of small crystals, but sometimes just plain rocks or even some with elaborate designs painted on them, Paradox Stones each hold a point of Quintessence inside it, and when crumbles, this Quintessence coun- ters a point of Paradox. Quintessence Absorbing Device (QAD) Technocracy: Progenitors pages 48 Though it can be easily mistaken for a normal piece of medical equipment, a QAD draws Quintessence from a living body hooked up to it and stores it for later use. It can draw three Quintessence per week from a Sleeper and five from a Mage, though the Mage loses one point of the Avatar background per week they are in a QAD. Tass Tapes

name: Parking Karma
description: A class of minor good luck charms, named for the ones used to find good parking spaces and avoid acci- dents. Minor wonders like these are relatively com- mon, and tend to help the user arrive at just the right time to take advantage of an opportunity. Sampo Fragments

name: Passion Tiki
description: Passion Tikis look much like fertility tikis, except the baby-like figure tends to have an impish grin and has an erect penis. These tikis are inhabited by spirits of lust and passion and, over the course of an hour, re- move inhibitions and encourage playful sexuality for those near them. PKD Paranoia Amplifier

name: Pattern-Ripping Claws
description: This tattoo allows the Mage to spend one point of Quintessence to do Aggravated damage in hand-to- hand unarmed combat. The tattoo often consists of some sort of claws (usually raptors or large cats) on their arms or legs. Anyone with Prime 1 or who suc- ceeds at Perception + Awareness (difficulty 7) can see that the owner’s hands and feet are covered by glowing energy claws. Shan Tattoo of Undisciplined Strength

name: PDNA
description: Pedagogical DNA is DNA imprinted with the in- formation desired, encoded in the base pairs, and when inserted into a Technocrat can teach them any- thing that is not of an occult nature (attempting this usually leads to madness). Most often, it is used for language and custom acquisition before going into the field. After an hour of preparation, the PDNA is in- jected into the Agent who then picks up the knowledge over several hours and retains it for roughly a week. The Piacenza Liver

name: Peacemaker
description: A special Colt .45 Peacemaker that fires bullets that pass through any non-living Matter. Bullets for this gun are hard to find, but when available do Damage 6 and has a difficulty of 7. Furthermore, the gun has a quick-draw effect that gives +3 to Initiative for the user. Raptor Silencer

name: Pele's Lamaku
description: Supernatural Perceiving things that others can’t is the most com- mon way for Mages to acquire information. The sim- plest techniques are available to even the newest ap- prentice. Sometimes, though, those perceptions need to be shared with someone who can’t do it, or more advanced techniques are needed. And for that, there are Wonders. Alley Shades Technocracy: Progenitors pages 48 Sunglasses specially treated to allow the wearer to use a Life 1/Correspondence 1 effect to learn the approx- imate location and movement of every lifeform in the area around them. Ear of Dionysus 48 Mystic Armory Made from kukui nuts and coconut leaves, this torch gives off a smokeless, yellow light when burned. When it is lit in the presence of a much larger fire, it triggers a Forces 2/Time 2 effect, showing images of the past or future in the flames. Prophetic Skull

name: Penance Bonds
description: Some Choristers, on their deathbeds, realize that they’ve done horrible things in their lives.
