To try to make right on the things they’ve done wrong, they sometimes have a Master of Spirit capture their spirit at the moment of death and forge them into a fetish. These fetishes are then given to those they wronged, or to a student, friend or ally who can right the wrongs they did. These fetishes can have virtually any power, and are almost exclusively made from Mages and Sor- cerers, though legends speak of others being forged into penance bonds. Space and Time travel and manipulation is one of the most dangerous and Vulgar areas of magick and binding the effects to Wonders helps mitigate that. Similarly, strange warpings of space can also be tied to Wonders to make the Paradox easier to manage and to give easi- er access to higher end techniques to less advanced Mages. Auspicious Sistrum

name: Perfected Focus
description: This is an object pulled down from the epiphanies, which is fully and completely the platonic ideal of its type. Such a sword could not act as a bludgeon or paperweight, for instance, but it will be exceptional at cutting flesh. The cost is determined by the use of the item and is at the Storyteller’s discretion. Sorcerous Tiki

name: Performance Blade
description: Similar to the replacement legs available to runners among the Sleepers, these are made of materials that they don’t have access to. For each point of Quintes- sence spent, the Scientist gains a burst of speed equal to their running speed. They can do this multiple times, but beyond the first they take a point of Lethal damage due to the stresses it puts on the rest of the body. Personal Cerebral Translation Unit

name: Perimeter Alarm
description: A collection of grid posts that can be set up around 10 foot by 10 foot area, the Perimeter Alarm detects any lifeforms or extra-dimensional entities that cross the boundary. This effect requires two Quintessence per hour to charge it. Whenever anything crosses the barrier, the Arete of the device is rolled as Perception to detect it, and an alarm sounds with a single success. The base model contains Spirit Sight and Sense Life, 140 Mystic Armory while a higher-level version adds one of Matter Percep- tion, Sense Thoughts or Time Sense. The Ragnaroc Home Security System

name: Personal Cerebral Translation Unit
description: A pill that releases tiny modules that find their way to the brain, once established in the body, it allows the comprehension of over 60 human languages and 30 programming languages. In fact, it also allows, with mental command, the user to speak in any of those languages at all. This can also translate into and be- tween programming languages. Extended use causes migraines. The device’s Arete is rolled to activate trans- lation which lasts for five days. Pincer Tool

name: Personality Software
description: Sometimes you just need to be someone else, tempo- rarily. The Personality Software suite can rewrite the user’s personality with another one temporarily, so that they can mimic them. The original personality re- emerges with the expenditure of 1 point of Willpower. This effect lasts longer the more similar the personality is to the owner’s as per the chart below. To create the personality of a specific person, several hours of sur- veillance footage is needed for analysis beforehand. Similarity Maximum Duration Very Similar 1 Month Sometimes Similar 3 Weeks Somewhat Different 2 Weeks Very Different 1 Week Diametrically Opposed 1 Day Physiognomizer

name: Physiognomizer
description: Based on the theory of physiognomy (that facial and body shape dictates personality, an essential aspect of Scientific Racism) the Physiognomizer alters appear- ance in order to alter personality, something like an Etherite version of the New World Order’s Room 101. It’s a ten foot by seven foot by three foot box with a blinking lights and a clear area just big enough to fit a person’s head. When active, the alterations being made will cause pain in a conscious subject. The sub- ject may resist with Willpower, but simple Mind mag- ick cannot undo it, Life magick is needed as well. Subliminal Broadcaster

name: Piacenza Liver, The
description: A bronze sheep’s liver that was created to teach ha- ruspexy by the ancient Etruscans. For anyone who speaks Etruscan, using the Liver as a guide decreases the difficulty of haruspexy by 2. Rapid Text Stream Data Reader

name: Pincer Tool
description: A replacement limb, usually for arms but sometimes for legs, consisting to a pair of two-foot metal jaws capable of grasping. It has an effective Strength of 5 and any time Strength is rolled where grip is essential- 66 Mystic Armory ly, Quintessence may be exchanged for automatic suc- cesses. Pneumatic Arm

name: PKD Paranoia Amplifier
description: Designed as a countermeasure to the Cult of Ecstasy, the PKD Paranoia Amplifier takes the natural paranoia caused by taking a psychoactive substance and turns it up to 11. Anyone who stays within an affected area for 12 hours will start to experience hallucinations. The difficulty of a roll to resist is based on how guilty the person feels that they are: a clean conscience gives difficulty of 3, minor reason to be paranoid is 5, etc. Each day that they are effected, the difficulty increases by 1 to a maximum of 10. Prayer Beads

name: PlastiSkin
description: A Progenitor made plastic that nearly perfectly du- plicates skin. It can be used to form disguises very ef- fectively, and in fact contains a low-level Mind effect to make people overlook imperfections in the form. With a simple Arete roll, the Device becomes a perfect match and people are more likely to trust that the wearer is who they say they are. The Rebooter Self-Retrieval Bio-Printer

name: Plutonium Pill
description: A pill that uses radiation to boost the intelligence of the user. When taken, the pill’s Arete is rolled and every two successes add one to some Mental Attribute that lasts for 24 hours. If two or more are taken in a 48-hour period, the later doses last less time (20 hours, then 12, then 8 and then 2). Furthermore, after the third dose the brain and head start to swell into a lightbulb shape, and this gets more and more exagger- ated with each dose. Another side effect is an erosion of moral sense. And a final side effect is, of course, cancer. If ten doses are taken in a year, the effects start to become permanent, including permanent Paradox. Super-Steroids

name: Pluvius' Javelinman
description: A hut sized assemblage of crystal discs at various an- a gles, driving each other by frictions. Copper rods lie among and around these discs. When set into motion, the discs spin and accelerate, drawing in ambient Quintessence. After three turns of warm-up, it sparks and hisses and gives off an ominous feeling. All magick within 30 yards is at +1 difficulty. After five turns, the weather turns stormy, and after 10, lightning bolts strike at points designated by the operator. Primium Knuckles

name: Pneumatic Arm
description: Sometimes a little mechanical help is needed. In those cases, this biomechanism allows the host to exert extreme force with their arms. On a successful Arete roll (Forces 4), the arm can be used as a piledriver. In combat, it does Strength + 3 damage and, on three or more successes to hit, executes a Body Slam maneuver (Mage: The Ascension 20th Anniversary Edition page 423). If used to parry melee or brawl attacks and the opponent’s successes are reduced below zero, the arm breaks the weapon or does damage to the attacker. Primium Countermeasures Technocracy: Iteration X page 52 (as Primium Plating)

name: Pocket Poltergeist
description: A small black box that converts mental energy into kinetic energy. With a successful Arete roll, the user can mentally manipulate objects for two minutes per dot of Intelligence, but afterwards they need to rest. Primium Cuffs

name: Portable Manar
description: 50 Mystic Armory

name: Power Suit
description: Created by any of a number of Syndicate designers, these suits are made to order and tailored to the indi- vidual buying them (at truly outrageous prices). The suit keeps the user calm in even the worst situations and expresses dominance in social situations, resulting in adding its Arete to Social Dice Pools. Impressing the wearer is at +2 difficulty. Furthermore, through a Prime effect, the Power Suit recharges its Quintessence store via the awe that it inspires in others. Even a dam- aged Power Suit will do this. The R.U.N.T.I.S. Suit

name: Prayer Beads
description: Using a Mind 2 effect, the prayer beads radiate a sense of peace to those near the owner. The owner can hold the beads to sense all the living things in the sur- rounding area and, if they stay perfectly still, cause almost all of those things to ignore them. Siren's Scent

name: Predator's Pheromones
description: These give the Scientist the ability to emit phero- mones that will make the subject more docile. By spending a point of Quintessence, the difficulty of the target to resist the Scientist’s Social rolls increases by 3. If rejected, the Scientist overcompensates and all fail- ures in social situations count as botches. Prehensile Tail

name: Prehensile Tail
description: A thin, muscular tail resembling that of an alligator and usually gray or green. It has an effective Strength of 5 and can make a grappling attack each turn. If used with other actions, has a +2 difficulty. If Rejected, the tail is gone and balance becomes more difficult: any balancing failures are botches. Quantum Monitor

name: PRETI gun
description: The PRETI (Pandora's Random Electrical Transfer- ence Inducer) gun is a nub-nosed pistol with spikes around the barrel. It fires a blue sphere of energy that, on a hit (Arete difficulty 7) lashes out and connects the target with the nearest source of electricity, either draining it or drawing from it for five seconds if it is continually supplied. It has an access port to allow it to be taken into the Digital Web. In the Digital Web, the difficulty reduces to 6 and it does standard Forces damage (halved in a Restricted Sector). Peacemaker

name: Primium Blade
description: Usually a broadsword but sometimes other sorts, these blades are made out of Primium. They inflict Aggravated damage, are light weight and resilient. Some blades have extra abilities included in them, and only those have Quintessence stores. Senex's Blade

name: Primium Cuffs
description: Though they look like plastic zip ties, these are actu- ally steel laced with Primium, specifically designed to keep captured Mages and Enlightened Scientists in custody. They resist breaking with Strength 8 and use their Arete as countermagick against the captive if they try anything. The stored Quintessence can, one-for- one, be used to cancel successes from magickal effects of the captive, though at most 2 per effect. For twice the background cost, there is a stronger version than can spend three Quintessence per effect. Time A staff topped by three interlocking rings (which ei- ther represent Greed, Ignorance and Hatred or The Buddha, His Teachings and His Monks), when rattled, it wards off danger and summons benevolent things to the user’s aid. This takes the form of an Entropy 3/Mind 3 effect to attract friendly people to the user and repel unfriendly ones. Some add Spirit 3/Entropy 1 to do the same with spirits. Doc Eon's Time Watch

name: Primium Knuckles
description: Though they look just like regular brass knuckles, Primium Knuckles have a major advantage: the perfect balancing make them punch faster and harder and makes it more difficult to block and resist the attacks. The Purifier's Needles

name: Prodigy
description: The Prodigy is the name of a line of laptops created by Virtual Adepts, updated when necessary to main- tain the power ratio. This laptop has the computation- al power of a supercomputer, and can tap into any electronic storage system on the planet and retrieve information both from and about it, even if the system is not connected to the Internet. Furthermore, it can be used to slow down time around it, allowing the user (and those nearby) to accomplish more in a given time period than is normally possible. Stage II Power Glove

name: Professor Parallax's Displacement Device
description: Lucky Some of the oldest magickal items in the world are Lucky Charms. They just bring luck and good fortune, often in specific areas to their owners. Though most of them have similar mechanics, they are usually unique to the culture that they originate in. Fishbowl of Prosperity

name: Prophetic Skull
description: The head of a fallen friend or a defeated foe can be a potent magickal object, and in ancient Britain and Gaul, they were used for divination. They can be asked yes or no questions about the past or future, and a “yes” is delivered by the box it is in becoming lighter, a “no” by it becoming heavier. A warning: the head, if a their personality does not match well with the ques- tioner, have been known to lie. Sacred Bullhide

name: Protocol Beeper
description: A useful tool for Void Engineers that spend a lot of time outside of the standard Consensus, the Protocol Beeper scans the area for Sleepers and beeps if they're present before a Procedure is used. This effect rolls the Beeper's Arete at difficulty 5, and it beeps on a single success. Sensor Glasses

name: Purifier's Needles, The
description: Made from a silver alloy, these needles are designed to defeat spirits and possession. They need to be re- forged if they strike metal or stone, or if they fail to penetrate some sort of magick. They are hurled like spears with Strength + 1 damage and a default difficul- ty of 7. They also may be fired from a crossbow, can- non or otherwise, with normal damage for the weapon but capped at 8. The needle’s Arete can be rolled as countermagick against any Spirit or Forces effect to stop it. Qi Needler

name: Qi Needler
description: A silver teardrop with a grip and black spike, this weapon disrupts the body’s Etheric flow. It fires (a Dexterity + Firearms roll with a +3 dice bonus due to being a burst) the black spike via electromagnetic fields at almost the speed of sound, and it has three settings, all of which are more easily (double dice) soaked by armor. The first just fires a short burst of needles, puncturing the target, inflicting damage as per a Forces effect. The second setting charges the needles with the Resonance sleep-inducing meridians and chakras, do- ing unsoakable Bashing damage via Life 3. The third setting is designed for killing, and preventing Scientific healing, using Life 3 to do Aggravated damage by di- rect Pattern attack. Selective Mine

name: Quad-Leg System
description: Able to be hidden under loose pants, this modifica- tion replaces the Scientist’s legs with a set of four legs with claws on the ends. For gripping onto surfaces and resisting being lifted or otherwise moved, the Scientist has Strength 10. Furthermore, environmental penalties to movement speed are ignored and jumping heights and distances are doubled (quadrupled with the ex- penditure of Quintessence.). Skeletal Enhancement

name: Quantum Datacaster
description: What mystics ignorantly call the Law of Contagion, the Union knows as Quantum Entanglement. This is the fundamental science underlying the Quantum Datacaster, which uses Entanglement to teleport data from one Datacaster to a paired one at another loca- tion. This means perfect and nearly impossible to in- tercept communication between hubs anywhere in the universe, theoretically infinite area of effect for Data Procedures, and all sorts of other benefits that data movement can give. Quantum Field Inverter (QFI)

name: Quantum Monitor
description: A nanotech sensor array deployed inside some Pro- genitors. These allow remote monitoring of vital signs in a nearly unjammable way. Radical Pigment Alteration

name: QUEST Transport
description: A truly joint project between the Void Engineers, It- eration X and the Progenitors, the Quantum Utility Electro Somatic Transport is a one-passenger Universal craft running on a computation system based on func- tional human brains. How the brains are selected is usually a matter of finding subjects whose minds are in good shape but unable to communicate or even func- tion due to injury or illness (whether they are taken consensually is a question for the Storyteller) and are preserved by FACADE Engineers, wired into a net- work by Biomechanics, and then installed in the heart of the ship by the Void Engineers. These brains still have personhood, and usually receive conditioning and training from the New World Order before the Void Engineers proceed to train them in navigation, computer systems and engineering. During this pro- cess, the brain is wired into the mechanical functions of the ship (which often include robotic arms for ef- fecting repairs) and communications, so that they can interact with the pilot and manage the ship should the long exposure to Deep Space cause damage to the pi- lot's psyche. Qui La Machinae X156

name: Qui La Machinae X156
description: The X156 is one of the Void Engineers’ best combat ships. Like the X200, it is all sharp edges and corners and coated in Primium to help it withstand the various dangers of the Deep Universe. At about 30 meters in length, the X156 is the small- er of the two models. They come equipped with three AFCs, six smaller cannons, a single PAD collector, and two ectoplasmic disruptors, the X156 has a set of large Quintessence batteries that it puts all captured energy into for later use. The armor on the hull gives six dice 122 Mystic Armory of soak and the hulls themselves have ten Health Lev- els before they are breached, and the Primium deducts three successes from all attempts to supernaturally affect the ship or its crew. Additionally, it contains a full sensor array (first level of each Sphere) that can be used with a dice pool of 6 against a difficulty of 6. The X156 requires at least three Enlightened crew mem- bers and usually will include two more for redundancy, along with 20-30 marines and up to 8 technicians. Qui La Machinae X160

name: Qui La Machinae X160
description: Some of the largest ships currently in operation, the X160 is a full 70 meters long and is often described as looking like "an insect bristling with guns and blades." The can hold thirty crew members, four officers and even have a stateroom. Their cargo capacity it 480 cubic meters. It's hull gives it 30 dice of armor and 10 dice of Primium, plus 102 Health levels and a Deep Universe rating for the life-support. The STAR-TPU is one of the best the Union has designed and capable of escape velocity on its own and a Deep Universe capa- ble Voidcast Drive. Its considerable weapons systems include (but are not necessarily limited to) six Alpha Class particle beams (9 dice of Lethal damage with a Perception + Energy Weapons roll), three Beta Class particle beams (15 dice of Lethal damage with a Per- ception + Energy Weapons roll), three HEX Missile volleys (9 Lethal damage with a dice pool of 10), eight points to mount machine guns (15 damage, close range only) and an assault ram (16 Aggravated damage with a Wits + Melee roll). On top of all that, it has camou- flage and docking capabilities. Qui La Machinae X200 “Vader”

name: Qui La Machinae X200 “Vader”
description: Coated in Primium and all angles to ward off any- thing from getting too close, the X200 was the most dangerous ship in the Void Engineer fleet. When a Node or Chantry needs to be cut off from the rest of the world until it can be sanitized, the X200 is called in. The X200 is larger, sometimes called a "Vader." It is almost 50 meters long, and significantly more expen- sive to make than the X156. They carry two batteries of three AFCs, eight smaller guns, two Ectoplasmic Dis- rupters, three PAD collectors, sensors (as the X156), and a hull as tough as the X156's. It can carry 50 ma- rines and 10 technicians, while requiring an Enlight- ened crew of five. There are, however, bugs that re- main: the X200 energy shields are faulty so that some- times it subtracts two successes (in addition to the Primium armor's three) from attacks against it and sometimes it just doesn't. Of much greater concern, though, is that it will sometimes fail to open a dimen- sional gateway large enough for it to pass through, causing the Voidship to cut itself apart on the opening. Sentinel Satellite

name: Quintessence Absorbing Device (QAD)
description: These mix tapes can be used to transfer Tass be- tween Hollow Ones (with Prime 3) along with secret information via the rote Thought/Sound Transfer. Vending Machines (Chi Restoration)

name: R.U.N.T.I.S. Suit, The
source_book: Tradition Book: Sons of Ether (1st Edition)
description: Rotham’s Umbral Navigation, Transportation and Illumination System. It is a full metal suit that looks like a cross between the robot from Forbidden Planet and a diving suit. It is airtight with an internal food and water supply, and is sealed airtight. The wearer gains 4 soak dice but gets +2 difficulty on all Dexterity rolls. The suit has an effective strength of 6, regardless of what the user has. It’s main effect, though, is Step- ping Sideways, and it contains a failsafe that if the user attempts to exit the suit while in the Umbra, it returns them to the physical world. At a cost of one quintes- sence per turn, it can fly as though using a jet pack. Ravana’s Skin

name: Radical Pigment Alteration
description: By splicing chameleon DNA into an Agent, they can change the color of their skin easily, adding four dice to disguise, camouflage and stealth rolls. An unfortu- nate side effect is that the skin will change colors slightly under stress, in a way similar to a mood ring, but this only affects the undertones. Sense Amplifiers

name: Robes of Blessing
description: Traditionally made out of priestly robes, now these are often made of suits or other professional clothing, but are still only given or made for members of the Celestial Chorus. The wearer gains the Iron Will merit and an effective dot in both Expression and Perfor- mance, an Influence of 2, plus an automatic Resources of 1 as no one is going to ask them to pay for low-cost items. It projects an aura of holiness, and a Charisma + Expression roll (difficulty 6) followed by an Arete roll (difficulty 7 - successes on Expression roll) will per- suade people of the rightness of the wearer's cause. One success makes their point of view seem reasona- ble, but three or more can lead those listening to be- lieve anything. The cost of wearing the robes is a gaeas, that if the wearer harms or refuses aid to an innocent or a loyal Chorister in need of help, the robes will fall off of them and cannot be worn again until they con- fess their sin to a senior Chorister and receive absolu- tion. The Robes of the Golden Mandarin

name: Schrödinger’s Closet
description: Vehicles By land, sea and air, Mages have always crafted vehi- cles that can go places and get there faster than Sleep- ers are able to. And when they travel by means that are like what is available more widely, they give their vehi- cles special properties that make them much, much better than the supposedly “equivalent” Sleeper tech- nology. ARC-2 Only useful away from the prying eyes of Sleepers, Schrödinger’s Closet is a doorway that an Agent can pass through and end up elsewhere, usually a Horizon Construct.

name: Shattered Lens
description: These are special glasses that help find ghosts and spirits. The wearer rolls Arete (difficulty 4) to see ghosts or spirits or to detect possession. Silver Strand

name: Shotgun Microphone
description: This microphone is designed to allow it to pick up sounds from a specific spot some distance away, espe- cially with the optional parabolic attachment. It can record audio from a location that is in the general area, though it need not have immediate sensory ac- cess. Sin-TV

name: Shuriken Glove
description: A glove, usually either fingerless and made of cotton or silk or else long black leather, with a sigil on the palm. This allows the wearer to focus their chi into a projectile weapon. With a Dexterity + Firearms roll the shuriken hits, and then the glove's Arete is rolled at difficulty 6 to do standard Forces damage. Multiple shuriken can be thrown, but each after the first sub- tracts one from the attack roll and adds one to the damage roll. Sword of Discharge

name: Silver Fan
description: This silver-painted fan gives the user powerful pro- tections against magick. For one Quintessence, it adds four to a countermagick roll. For two Quintessence, it adds four dice to the countermagick roll and if success- ful reflects the effect back at the caster. Ward Tiki

name: Silver Strand
description: This is the preserved silver cord of some Umbral traveler, and appears as a frayed cord of a gossamer silvery material. These are only useful to a Mage who is Astral projecting, and can be used to give their own silver thread four dice of armor. Alternatively, if at- tached to a soulless living body, it can be offered to allow a High Umbral entity to possess a body for as long as a lunar month, a service considered valuable by many Umbral beings. Spirit Door

name: Siren's Scent
description: Though it comes in hundreds of variations, this charm takes the form of a potion, salve, powder or perfume with any of a wide variety of scents. When used, it adds four dice to all Social dice pools. Once whatever emotions are being invoked set in, those around the wearer will follow through unless they succeed at a Willpower roll at difficulty 7. Mental Manipulation 87 88 Mystic Armory Perceptions Though supernatural perceptions are handled else- where, here we discuss Wonders that alter normal perceptions. These may give the user (or target) hallu- cinations, may translate languages, or may even make it look like there’s nothing out of the ordinary some- where even though it was just the scene of a major battle in the Ascension War. Consensual Hallucination Generator

name: Siren's Tears and the Breather Collar
description: Created by the Fallen to allow access to one of their Labyrinths, these two Devices are designed to allow them to breathe long enough underwater to get to the pocket of air containing the Labyrinth itself. Siren’s Tears resemble the bubble that certain water spider’s fill up with air. It’s membrane keeps the water out and by drawing in or expelling air it can control the depth that the user has sunk to. One of these bub- bles can hold as many as four people, though it takes a certain kind of madness to climb into a bubble for an undersea trip. The Breather Collar, meanwhile, is a leather collar that injects probes into the wearer’s jugular. Through these probes, it takes over the process of oxygen and waste exchange with the water, allowing the wearer to breathe underwater. Of course, this happens at the neck, so if the wearer attempts to breathe directly, they Space The Deep Umbra is an inhospitable place, and the outer Solar System even more so. Yet, Mages have been at the forefront of exploration and even fighting over territory far away from the Earth. Though this travel is not restricted to the Void Engineers, this sec- can still drown. Breathing is best accomplished by the wearer holding their breath. In either case, the device works after an Arete roll (difficulty 6) succeeds, it failed on a failure…and it fails partway through the journey on a botch. Temporal Transit Converter (TTC)

name: The Six Seals of Ganzir
description: A book about six powerful demons along with rites for summoning, binding and banishing them. It can be found in Latin and Arabic, though it is originally in Babylonian. It can act as the Library background up to level 4, and can teach the Cosmology, Enigmas, Lore (spirits), Metaphysics and Occult Knowledges. The Uranian Pleasure Manual

name: Shifter's Skin
description: By transplanting the skin from a shapeshifter, the Scientist gains the ability to change the relevant part of their body at a cost of one Quintessence. If Rejected, this area has no soak dice until healed. Trollhide

name: Sleepwalker's Drum
description: Emotions Even beginners with Mind magick can influence the emotions of others, and Wonders tend to give them wider reach, more control, and often the ability to alter them farther than they otherwise could. Block Party Videos

name: Slip-Rex, The King of Lubricants
description: This lubricant comes in an old-fashioned oil can with a yellow and blue label. Any surface that it touch- es becomes perfectly frictionless, making standing, much less walking, nigh impossible. The can can be refilled with any sort of lubricant and the user rolls their Arete (difficulty 5) to convert it into Slip-Rex. The Testing Flask

name: The Sorcerer's Apprentice
description: Also called “The Mentor” or, less respectfully, “The Speak-n-Spell,” the Sorcerer’s Apprentice was devel- oped by House Thig and allows even inexperienced Hermetics to use complex rituals and designs by pro- jecting them directly into the user’s mind. The name “The Sorcerer’s Apprentice” stuck as a fitting warning to Hermetics that overreliance on it could bring trou- Books and Learning 17 ble: knowing what to do may not give great insight into why and when to do it. Due to these dangers, it is usually used now as teaching tool, to allow apprentices the ability to prac- tice with complex rituals more quickly than they oth- erwise would be able to. The biggest downside is that this creates the knowledge of how to do a ritual, and if that becomes corrupted the only way to fix it is to use the device again, particularly as the user doesn’t have the knowledge to solve the problem themselves. When used, the Storyteller makes a secret roll of the character’s Intelligence + Occult (difficulty 8). There are three possible outcomes: • On a success, the Hermetic gains access to the Holy Pentacles rote even without the appropriate Spirit or Arete rating. For each success on the roll, they gain a dot of Spirit Lore. These are permanent, but the dots are only applied in intuitive situations, the Mage comes across as ignorant and inarticulate in learned discussions. 18 Mystic Armory • On a failure, something has been learned but it is not right. What has gone wrong is up to the Story- teller, and it might be that the rote just doesn’t a work, but it might also be successful summoning and no knowledge of how to handle the spirit once it arrives. • On a botch, the Hermetic is confident that they have everything right, and even succeed at a sum- mons. Though what shows up is another matter, and it will not be friendly, happy, or bound by pro- tocol when encountering the Hermetic. To use the Sorcerer’s Apprentice requires that either the user or an ally roll Intelligence + Occult (difficulty 7) and Wits + Hacking (if not a member of House Thig/Verditius). They then fall into a coma for 48 hours, after which the information has been transmit- ted. To erase a bad experience requires that it all be done again. If the Mage fails to learn after four tries, they lose two permanent points of Occult, as they can no longer tell good information from bad. Woodblock of Auspicious Formulae

name: Sorcerous Tiki
description: Relatively small at only about 10 inches, a sorcerous tiki is almost naturally proportioned, but with a head that is slightly too large. The heads have large eyes and jagged teeth and, most disturbingly of all, real human hair. By making a blood sacrifice to the spirit within the tiki right before performing some other magick, the mage can decrease the Correspondence range to target. This ritual requires a lot of blood: six blood points to decrease the difficulty of the Arete by -3 (-1 per two points), 12 more to reduce the Correspond- ence range (by one step per two points) and then an additional die on the Arete roll is gained for each ten points spent in total. Note that an adult human has 10 blood points, three can be lost without damage and after that it is one Health Level of unsoakable damage per point. Tempest Hardening 38 Mystic Armory

name: Soul Mates
description: A powerful Chorister may, upon finding a child with an unusual destiny, can call into existence a Soul Mate for the child. This will cause a second child to be con- ceived whose fate will be bound to the first. The Soul Mate will never Awaken, but may become a psychic or sorcerer, or have True Faith. Though many factors go into the relationship between the child and the Soul Mate, the child gains the True Love merit, the Soul Mate will be either a living relic or a periapt whose abilities can only be used in the presence of the child. The Staff of Heralds

name: Space Jam
description: This wrist unit blocks out all satellite surveillance within a 10-foot radius. Best of all, the interference it produces is normal enough that it doesn’t send up red flags that cloaking is being done. Talisman of the Mask

name: Spear of Gobhniu
description: Gobhniu was the Irish god of smiths, and his spears never miss their marks: they gain two dice on attack rolls and do Aggravated damage. Three Pearls of Thunder and Lightning

name: Spectre Limousine
description: Convention Book: New World Order page 82 (Mark 3) The black limousine the size of a luxury sedan (or more rarely a full limousine) that is famous for its use by the Men in Black. It can hold several upgrades, listed below. Of course, misuse of a Spectre Limousine will have it returned to the Union’s motor pool. • Smoke Cloud Arete 3, Quintessence 15, Background Cost 6 The car releases a black cloud of smoke, Will- power is needed for pursuers to continue following. • Nitro-Quintessence Injector Arete 4, Quintessence 20, Background Cost 8 By injecting Quintessence directly into the en- gine, this Device makes an Arete roll (difficulty 5) and increases the speed of the car. This caps at double the car’s usual top speed, but this is rarely done because the driver must still maintain control of the car. • Vehicular Manar • Bootlegger Jets Arete 5, Quintessence 25, Background Cost 8 These small jets are used to allow the vehicle to do a 180 degree turn at any speed. • Energy Shielding Arete 4, Quintessence 20, Background Cost 6 or 8 By lacing the body of the Spectre with Primi- um, it gains three dice of countermagick. The more expensive variant increases the number of coun- termagick dice by one by sealing up the windows with Primium when a button is pressed. • Ejector Seat Arete 4, Quintessence 20, Background Cost 8 This, of course, ejects the driver. The seat, for- tunately, comes with a parachute. Transit and Communication 129 The upgraded version, the Mark 3, can additionally have the following upgrades • Chameleon Circuit Arete 2, Quintessence 10, Background Cost 4 This upgrade allows the Spectre to change its appearance to anything, from a hearse to a military vehicle, police cars or old clunkers. • Fast and Furious Arete 2, Quintessence 10, Background Cost 4 For one turn per point of Quintessence spent, the Spectre can move at double speed. Of course, this increases the damage done in a crash… • Drift Kit Arete 3, Quintessence 15, Background Cost 6 At a cost of one Quintessence, this upgrade al- lows the Spectre to execute an immediate 90-degree turn, and for three it can do 180. • High-Energy Shielding Arete 4, Quintessence 20, Background Cost 8 Ceramic and Primium shielding protects the Spectre and those inside. This provides four dice of countermagick (doubled for a point of Quintes- sence), and this causes the chassis to crackle with blue electricity. • Holdout Chaingun Arete 4, Quintessence 20, Background Cost 8 A weapon that pops up out of a panel hidden under the hood, it can fire in a 90 degree arc facing forward from the front of the Spectre. For one point of Quintessence, it will fire about thirty bul- lets. If neither the driver nor any passenger is con- trolling it, it fires with an auto-targeting system that has seven dice to hit, and causes six dice of Lethal damage on a hit. It is a fully automatic weapon. • Contingency Plan Arete 6, Quintessence 30, Background Cost 10 This upgrade is useful as a last-ditch defense: by spending ten Quintessence, plus five for each passenger, the Spectre can teleport back to a Divi- sion Q laboratory. Anyone left behind is still sub- ject to conservation of momentum, so it’s best to be careful to make sure anyone that the driver wants to keep around is included in this effect. 130 Mystic Armory Submersible Car

name: Spirit Door
description: Extremely rare items that only came into existence after the Avatar Storm, they are hand-woven wool car- pets usually 1 yard by 2-3 yards. The centers contain designs in earth tones and the border pattern resem- bles a doorway. This gives the user access to the stronger version of the Stormwarden merit, allowing them to ignore the Avatar Storm entirely, when using the Spirit Door to Breath The Gauntlet. The Door only works if the Gauntlet is 6 or less, and then rolls Arete for the Breach the Gauntlet effect. It exists on both sides of the Gauntlet, and so the user can pull the carpet through the doorway it creates behind them. Spirit Goggles

name: Spiritus Pastille
description: A small tablet that, when lit, will emit a fragrant smoke. The smoke from this pastille will be gray and smell of cloves and patchouli. It reveals ghosts and their general inclination as well as attracting them. Through the smoke, benevolent ghosts will appear silvery gray and Spectres black. Voice Disguiser

name: Staff of Heralds, The
description: The Traditions have long had Heralds and Messen- gers who had to travel to bring news and communica- tion between Chantries. For a long time, the only guarantee of the authenticity of the news was trusting the Herald's word. After this became untenable, the Euthanatos created the Staff of Heralds. Whoever has it can use it to mark a Herald's Fate so that anyone with the first rank of Entropy can see that they've been so marked. Unfortunately, the Staff has been lost and may be destroyed. The Tenth Seat

name: Stage II Power Glove
description: Sometimes, it’s best not to carry your whole comput- er system with you. For those times, the Power Glove is popular: a computer console that wraps around the user’s forearm, the display is millimeter thick plastic, and the unit has two wires that can connect to any port. It acts fully as a portable Trinary computer. To connect the universal wires it requires an Arete roll against difficulty 6 (7 with Witnesses). TWURP

name: STAR Units
description: Proprietary for the Void Engineers and so not avail- able to other Conventions, the STAR (Stellar Tass Augmentation Refinery) Unit breaks down material from stars or interstellar dust and converts it to Quin- tessence. At least one STAR Unit is attached to every Voidship, and many large Void Engineer Constructs are powered by several STAR Units. Tass-Powered Propulsion Units (TPUs)

name: Subdermal Transponder
description: A micro-transmitter and receiver, usually embedded into the back of the neck or under the arm. When pressed, the Agent can send secure signals virtually anywhere on Earth, and it will pick up anything within about three feet. The higher cost version isn’t limited to Earth, but can transmit into other dimensions when necessary. Internal 67 68 Mystic Armory Sub-Dermals

name: Subliminal Broadcaster
description: This device is designed to send out subliminal com- mands, with a cost of 4 for emotions, 6 for infor- mation and 8 for commands. It can be resisted actively with Willpower, but Perception + Awareness or magick is necessary to even know it is being used. Any sort of method of transmitting the information can be made into a subliminal broadcaster, with historical ones including tape players, stereos and hidden speakers. Thought Transference Device

name: Sword of Discharge
description: An always sharp katana that does Strength + 5 dam- age at difficulty six. Furthermore, when it strikes, it can discharge electricity as a Forces 2 effect. Swords of Mars

name: Swords of Mars
description: The finest examples of swordsmithing from the Or- der of Hermes, these blades are made of purified iron with astrological sigils etched into them. As such, they are highly prized both in and out of the Order. All attack, parry and damage rolls have their difficulty reduced by 3 and increase the target's soak roll difficul- ty by 3. Its Arete can be rolled (difficulty 5) to add Forces damage to an attack at a cost of one point of Quintessence. The majority have a gaeas which causes Weapons 137 the Sword to lose its bonuses if the owner fails to act as a "just and honorable" champion of the Traditions. Sword-Breaker

name: Syringe Pharmacopeia
description: Activated by application to a wound, these specially- treated cloths can heal wound quickly, saving the user's life in a dire emergency. Master Joro's Sash

name: Talisman of the Mask
description: One of the best defenses against discovery the Solifi- cati have produced, this Talisman takes the form of a coin worn around the neck. When wearing it, Mind magick will not reveal them as anything other than a Sleeper. They can also use it to cause them to appear differently through a minor illusion. allow foresight (or at least, seeing probable futures) or else improve the Mage’s ability to perceive the hidden patterns that underly everything, and thus can under- stand what is going to happen and even why. Divining Staff

name: Tass Tapes
description: These mix tapes can be used to transfer Tass be- tween Hollow Ones (with Prime 3) along with secret information via the rote Thought/Sound Transfer. Vending Machines (Chi Restoration)

name: Tass-Powered Propulsion Units (TPUs)
description: Teleportation Though many aspects of transport have become ac- cepted into the Consensus, teleportation has not, de- spite a consistent propaganda push in science-fiction to Voidships of all sorts require engines. This is where TPUs come in: they convert large quantities of Tass into thrust. There are many designs, going back to internal combustion to modern solar sails. However, this requires a massive amount of Tass, and the amount contained internally only lasts about a day. Most of them are paired with STAR Units. Tranquility Raptor Class Corvette

name: TDR "Living" Computer and Video Games
description: Hyperrealistic video games that train quick and out- side-the-box thinking skills in those who play them. Their online modes often put Sleeper players into contract with Virtual Adepts, making them a very ef- fective recruitment tool. Thought Programs

name: TDSPV
description: These vehicles are rare, but are among the most se- cure methods of transport that the Union has to offer, and so are available to anyone sufficiently high up in need of them. The passenger compartment of the TDSPV (Trans-Dimensional Superior Protective Vehi- cle) is completely sealed from the outside world: in fact, it is in a pocket Realm that cannot easily be ac- cessed. The drive of the limousine it mimics can only speak to the passenger via an intercom. The TDSPV requires a point of Quintessence for every 10 km trav- eled, and is usually coincidental. When the doors are closed, Arete is rolled against a difficulty of 7. Success means everything worked as intended, failure leaves the passengers physically in the car. A botch can lead to some severe complications, as can the use of magick or Enlightened Science within the compartment. Vehicular Satellite Uplink

name: Telepathy Specs
description: Though they only work for children and the similar- ly open-minded, these plastic glasses allow the wearer to see a visual representation of the strongest surface thoughts they have, nothing deep or secret, though. Time-Divider

name: Telescoping Limb
description: Fitted with an internal system of hoses, pulleys, valves and pistons, this enhancement allows a Scientist to extent their reach significantly beyond their normal limits, up to ten feet for arms and six feet for legs. It takes a full turn to extend or retract unless a Quintes- sence is spent. Due to the awkwardness that most peo- ple find with them, using them in combat when ex- tended causes a +2 penalty to difficulty. Visual Data & Analysis Spectrum (VDAS)

name: Tempest Hardening
description: A modification that can be added to almost any de- vice, it ensures that the device’s electronics cannot be interfered with through supernatural means. Though stronger and weaker versions exist, the normal is for five dice of countermagick. The Thief's Claw

name: Temple of the Theoi Cthon, The
description: Some periapts are too large to carry, and the Temple is one such. It is a mysterious building sitting on a 1- point Node in Greece. It resembles other ancient Greek temples and has a complex system of tunnels and caverns underneath it that seem rather out of place in Greece. Though it stores a large amount of Quintessence, it is a strange sort and most Mages can’t figure out how to make effective use of it, which has resulted in the Temple remaining unclaimed, with a bit of help from the Temple’s Arcane rating of 4. Only seven Folding Gates exist, and no one is cer- tain how to make more. The ones that exist are centu- ries old. They appear as small metal boxes made of rods about the size of a baseball, and unfold (with an Intelligence + Engimas roll at difficulty 7) into a 3 foot by 7 foot gateway that can stand on its own. This rec- tangle acts as a gateway (with an Arete roll for the Tal- isman with difficulty 7 minus number of successes on the previous roll) to a two-room sanctum, one is a workroom and the other a bedroom and both have a comfortable temperature, fresh air and water, and Primal Energy 113 114 Mystic Armory plumbing. Outfitted properly, they can hold 2-3 peo- ple indefinitely or up to 8 for a (cramped) night or two. The gate can be folded up from the inside and locked so that anyone investigating without at least Correspondence 4 will not be able to determine the nature of the box or gateway. Finally, in the bedroom is a 2 point Node. Ether Tracking Clockwork Wonder Globe

name: Temporal Transit Converter (TTC)
description: An EXTREMELY unstable device not to be used outside of carefully controlled circumstances! The TTC consists of a circle of electronics components wired to a controller which allows the Void Engineer to transport themselves to another time. Unfortunate- ly, it is fairly common for the time traveler to either be stranded or destroyed. Fixing this is an active area of research. Wings of Icarus

name: Tenth Seat, The
description: In Horizon, a chair was put at the Council's table for each Sphere. A Tenth had long been reserved, for when the Tenth Sphere was truly discovered, as many Traditions believed there is one. At one point, a sigil appeared on the Seat, but despite all attempts, no one could decipher it. In the wake of the destruction of Horizon, the mysterious Tenth Seat has gone missing, even from the ruins. Miscellaneous 99 100 Mystic Armory Other Beings Mages deal with non-human creatures all the time, so it is hardly surprising that they create Wonders deal- ing with them. Of course, some of those Wonders ARE non-human beings, or at least are life of some sort by many reasonable definitions. Artificial This section concerns artificial life and intelligence in all its forms. Whether it is intelligence software created by the Virtual Adepts, bizarre creatures of the Progenitors, or robots built by the Society of Ether. Battle Homunculus

name: Testing Flask, The
description: Invented by the Solificati, this Flask allows the user to determine if a given liquid is poisonous or toxic. Either way, the user can cause non-toxic substances to become tainted or purify toxic ones. In fact, up to five gallons of the liquid can be tainted or purified by pouring the contents of the flask into a container of the same liquid. Simple poisons and acids require two successes, complete ones require three. Creation and Destruction Items that can create something from nothing, as well as those that can destroy things completely and utterly (even if they aren’t weapons) are prized among Mages of all sorts. A particularly popular class of such things are wonders that create money as needed, so that the Mage is never actually broke. Correlli's Badass Jackhammer

name: Theresita's Thousand Magick-Finger Bed
description: A massage bed designed by a member of the Cult of Ecstasy to be able to keep up with her, whatever her mood. It can massage gently during relaxation time and the magic fingers will kick in when the Cultist so desires. The beds worth 6 background points also have a power the doesn’t mimic the mundane: time dilation. On a successful Arete roll, it causes time to pass at 10 times the usual rate in the bed, allowing a full night’s sleep in less than an hour, among other things. Special Items

name: Thief's Claw, The
description: An odd contraption made of wood, lodestone and various alloys, it folds out to reveal a massive array of probes, pins and keys. With these, the interior of mechanisms can be manipulated without having to take them apart. Probing costs no Quintessence, but a attempting to trigger the mechanism costs one point. Trance Drum

name: Thirsty Blade of Kali
description: Though they come in many shapes, these blades are all made of black iron and steal the Quintessence from those it is used to attack. Some Euthanatoi view them as fundamentally corrupted by Jhor, but others believe that there is no reason to waste the Quintessence that animates a person when giving the Good Death to someone. Powered by Prime and a bound spirit, the blade does Strength + 1 Aggravated damage and pulls two points of Quintessence (up to the damage inflicted) from the target. The spirit (which need not be related to Kali) cleans most of the negative resonance from the Quin- tessence before passing half of it along to the Mage. Tradition Blades

name: Thought Programs
description: Thought Programs are programs that attach to the Mage’s icon and grant an ability at 5 while in the Digi- tal Web. Physical talents cannot be granted if the Mage is Holistically Immersed, as the physical body is not just a mental construct. Hardware When mundane computers just won’t do the trick, Enlightened and Enchanted hardware can be the an- swer. These are either beyond the capabilities of mod- ern computers, made portable when an equivalent computer should not be, or add capabilities that nor- mal computers would never manage, such as storing rotes for later use and mitigating paradox from ex- treme computation. 24 Mystic Armory The Bond Watch

name: Three Pearls of Thunder and Lightning
description: A silk pouch containing three near-perfect pearls. Each pearl has a different effect that can be used once: when used the pearl is thrown and destroyed, and this is accompanied by a thunderclap and lightning flash. Further, they depend on a successful Dexterity + Melee roll at difficulty 7, 4 if thrown at ground. • An explosion, doing 5 Aggravated damage to every- one within 10 feet • Put a spirit to sleep if hit with pearl, draining 20 points of Power • Turns a living target to stone (Life 4/Matter 2) for one week/success on the Pearl’s Arete roll, difficul- ty 8. War Tiki

name: Tide Jewel
description: 80 Mystic Armory Supposedly used by the tide dragons of Japan and China to control the tides, several of these perfect spheres of blue or green crystal have found their way into the hands of Mages. When within sight of the ocean, the Mage may spend the Quintessence stored in the Jewel. For each point spent, the tide can be raised or lowered by one foot per minute for five miles around the user. This lasts for up to one hour for each dot the user has in Forces plus one hour per point of Quintessence spent to make it last. Using a Tide Jewel takes a toll on the user, costing one point of Quintes- sence of theirs for each point of the Jewel’s that is spent. Wolf Link

name: Time-Divider
description: A fist sized mechanism in a brass case, this is an ex- tremely accurate clock. However, it doesn’t have a clock face as modern users would be accustomed to, but rather something more unique and each one will be different. Trushades

name: Tonics and Potions
description: Herbal or alchemical mixes, tonics and potions can have a wide variety of uses. The most common ones, though, are healing and physical enhancement. Twilight Balm

name: Torc of Donn
description: Made by the Aided to aid in trips to the Celtic un- derworld, Tech Duinn, these are torcs of gilt whale- bone with carved knotwork representing the trip to the underworld. As Tech Duinn is the Land Under the Waves, the torc allows the wearer to breathe underwa- ter as well as move through it easily. It also allows fully submerged wearer to pass into the Underworld through a (relatively) safe path. No one knows how to make them anymore. The last one was made in 1876 by an Aided who only believed in keeping an oral tradition, and the technique was not passed on. Zulu Warshield 162 Mystic Armory a

name: Totem Tattoo
description: Often considered the peak of mystic tattoo artistry, the Totem Tattoo binds the bearer to a spirit that it represents. This requires that the spirit be willing and allows the bearer to transform into an appropriate animal, representing that spirit. The 8-point version uses a vulgar Life 4/Spirit 2 effect to take their totem’s physical form, the 9-point version uses Spirit 3 to allow the Mage to also step sideways. The 10-point version, which is extremely rare, turns the user into a living fetish allowing the totem to work its power through the Mage. Because this is bound to the Mage’s pattern, they gain four or five points of permanent paradox, depending on which version of the tattoo they have. This replacement forearm is very good, particularly for the Dark Fantastick. It cannot be used for delicate work, but it can grip and punch and manage signals to and from the upper arm. On top of that, it has a fold- out blade that can be used as a dagger in combat. Cardio-Muscular Assemblage

name: Tradition Blades
description: A collection of nine blades each created by one of the Traditions. They are symbols of political power within the Traditions as well as weapons and their 138 Mystic Armory other powers. Anyone who holds the blade of a Tradi- tion will at the least be given some respect by the members of that Tradition, and much will be traded to recover it if it is in the hands of an outsider. Akashayana: A two-foot-long straight-bladed sword for thrusting called a jian, it's made out of a piece of steel etched with swirling dragons. The cross-guard is shaped like a pair of phoenixes (feng huang) in flight. A wielder can concentrate on it to force it to change shape into any weapon between two and four feet in length with a single blade. A properly trained Akashic can change the shape midfight, removing a success from an opponent's parry or dodge due to the sudden changing of the weight. Celestial Chorus: At four feet in length, this cruci- form sword isn't really a one-handed weapon, more of a hand and a half. The most significant thing about it, though, is that it is forged of Primium, allowing a Dex- terity + Melee (difficulty 6) parry against directed mag- ickal attacks. Cult of Ecstasy: A lotus sword from India, about two and a half feet long, the steel has been blued and it has a simple pommel with a gold foil covered cross-guard. It has a tendency of showing up at important events, bringing its owner along with it. The holder of the sword gains two ranks of the Destiny background while the sword belongs to them, often leading to them being in dangerous situations. Dreamspeakers: A plain obsidian blade, under a foot in length, the Dreamspeaker blade is chipped and worn by age. Though it looks unassuming, this fetish blade contains several spirits, causing it to have full effect on spirits, granting the ability to cut a hole in the Gauntlet anywhere with a rating of at most 3, staying open for two turns. Euthanatoi: This curved blade seems simple, but it automatically seeks out weak points, cutting through armor like paper. Its true power is as an instrument of justice. If it strikes a person who has broken a magickal oath, it kills them instantly, and no magick or defense can prevent it. Order of Hermes: A thin, double-edged medieval broadsword better for ritual work than for combat, the Hermetic blade lowers all magick difficulties by two while held. In Certamen (or its variants), it grants an automatic success each turn, and Hermetic tradition prevents anyone from denying its use in a Certamen challenge, though the other Traditions generally don't feel bound by this precedent. Society of Ether: When the need to craft Tradition Blades came up, the Etherites produced a very simple one. A Roman Gladius, but it has been reinforced in every way imaginable. No known force can break or even bend it. Verbena: The Verbena blade could never be any- thing other than an athame, the ceremonial knife. When a Verbena sacrifices their own health using this athame, they not only gain a point of Quintessence for each health level, but also an automatic success on the resulting effect. Virtual Adepts: As always, the Virtual Adepts have to be different. The Virtual Adept blade is non- physical, existing only in the Digital Web. The Datablade, as it is called, prevents the user from ever botching a Computer related roll. The Datablade ap- pears in the Digital Web as a xiphold weapon with a green grid structure, like unformatted web space. Fur- thermore, it can strike anyone in the Digital Web, even killing someone if they are holistically immersed. The Twins

name: Trance Drum
description: A drum with a hollow and resonance tone, it allows the user to enter a trance state almost instantly. When it is playing, anyone who hears it can enter a trance state with difficulty reduced to 3. Magick that uses trance states is done at a -1 difficulty as well. When a non-Mage is in its presence, they are more susceptible to suggestion (and Subterfuge) as well as Mind magick, giving all such rolls a -2 difficulty. A skilled drummer (Performance at least 3) can manipulate an audience a with this drum, triggering anything from an orgy to a brawl. Paradox accrued by this drum is suffered by a ran- dom Mage within 10 feet of it. Xenon Bulb

name: Oriole of Tranquility
description: A jeweled, clockwork oriole is helpful in a wide vari- ety of situations, from keeping people calm during negotiations to creating an environment conducive to studying. It has two levels of effect. The lower level, which costs one Quintessence for 10 minutes, decreas- es the difficulty of tasks requiring focus by one or more (determined by the storyteller). The higher level, which costs one Quintessence per turn in combat or one per 10 minutes outside it, affects everyone who can hear it and gives them peacefulness, tranquility and calm. Those affected cannot take any hostile actions without a Willpower roll (difficulty 10), and then all violent tasks have their difficulty increased by one, whereas peaceful actions have their difficulty decreased by one. Passion Tiki

name: Tranquility Raptor Class Corvette
description: Thirty-five meters long and shaped like a wedge, the Tranquility Raptor is a larger Voidship than a Void Engine, but still designed for a smaller crew than older designs. They have room for six crew members and two officers, plus 240 cubic meters of cargo. The life support system can handle Deep Universe travel, and it has 20 dice of armor and 34 health levels. It's STAR- TPU drive can manage escape velocity and it has a Voidcast drive capable of Deep Universe travel. It con- tains two Beta Class particle beams (15 dice of Lethal damage with dice pool of Perception + Energy Weap- ons), a Kinetic weapon (9 Lethal damage with Percep- tion + Helmsman + 10 dice pool) loaded with six shots, and 2 HEX Missile volleys (9 Lethal damage with a dice pool of 10). Void Engine Shuttlecraft

name: Transport Pass Card
description: When a Technocrat needs to travel a long distance in a hurry, the Transport Pass Card is their best friend. Though the path taken may be implausible or unusual, the Agent uses a touch interface on the back of the card and tells it where they need to go, and it will make all of the arrangements for the Agent to arrive there as quickly as possible. The Agent makes a Wits + Technology roll at difficulty 6 (7 if not traveling alone) and subtracts the successes from the same difficulty for an Arete roll for the card. Each success cuts the travel time in half. If this means that the only way to travel in that time is by parachuting out of an experimental military supersonic plane, then so be it. Generally, this will allow the Agent (and companions) to travel com- fortably (first class on planes, etc.) and removes all waiting time: they will walk off one vehicle and directly into the next. UniCash Card

name: Traveler's Charm
description: A priest or magicians blesses a small object making it into a charm. It allows a reroll on any one nonmagick roll up to three times, and then the magick of the charm fades. Witchward

name: Traveling Coat
description: Depending on era, these take the form of cloaks, overcoats or trench coats...or whatever else fashion dictates. The coat comes with many smallish benefits that aid in travel: resistance to cold, a bonus of two points to concealment of items due to the large num- ber of pockets, a die of armor, fire resistant (not proof!). The strongest of them, though, come with pockets larger on the inside, improvements to the fire resistance, and even some Arcane. Universal Suit

name: Trollhide
description: Internal 57 58 Mystic Armory On those rare occasions when Technocrats can ob- tain the skin of a troll, they can create this xenotrans- plant. It rarely covers the Scientist’s entire skin, but the parts where it does have inhuman coloration and tex- ture giving four dice of armor (which stacks with worn armor). The skin also regenerates a bashing level per turn or a lethal level every hour. Its Quintessence may be spent to regenerate a Lethal Health Level instead of a Bashing in one turn. Opponents may target uncov- ered parts at +2 difficulty. If Rejected, the areas cov- ered have no soak dice until repaired. Undead Strength

name: Trushades
description: Put out by Greer Laboratories (a joint Etherite, Ec- static and Virtual Adept group), these are glasses or goggles with up to three perception effects built into them. There are many varieties not directly from them with more generic names, but theirs has become the standard. The 1-3 effects built in can be any rank 1 visual per- ception Effect. These are usually activated by a touch (button) or an activation word. Anyone who attempts to use three such effects together suffers -2 to Percep- tion rolls and ends up with a nasty headache. Truth-Seeing Stone

name: Truth Serum
description: A quick-injector that, for the cost of one Quintes- sence, can allow a single statement to be implanted into an unEnlightened target that they will accept as the truth. This CAN be done multiple times, but each one after the first causes a level of Lethal damage to the subject. Against anyone capable of countermagick, they may use it as a defense, rolled against the Truth Serum’s Arete. Mental Manipulation 91 92 Mystic Armory Miscellaneous Some Wonders defy a strong classification. In this chapter, we cover a grab-bag of Wonders that fit no- where else, dividing them into subsections based on what commonalities they have. These subsections consist of Wonders that manipu- late Light and Sound, those that act as Lucky Charms, Light and These Wonders manipulate light and sound, creat- ing illusions and darkness, either for something useful or just for the aesthetic. 3-Dim Sonographic Sense Factory

name: Truth-Seeing Stone
description: A gem cut according to the laws of sacred geometry and attuned to a specific sphere and perception effect for that sphere. It then gives access to that perception effect by looking through the gem. Each use costs one Quintessence and when it runs out, the Stone becomes cloudy and opaque until recharged. Unstoppable Binoculars

name: Tsu-Ti (Divine Bamboo)
description: A stick of bamboo which must have been grown on a seashore. When a message is written in a Mage’s blood on a rock, the message will seep in and become unde- tectable. When a Mage Awakened through any sort of demonic pact touches the rock, the message will be revealed until contact is broken. Viasilicos Technomancer’s Toybox page 57 (as Lesser Viasilicos)

name: Tsukahara Shigekatsu's August Mirror
description: Several centuries old, the August Mirror is a mir- rored octagon that is 30 centimeters by 30 centimeters, and about one centimeter thick. It is decorated with Chinese ideograms and trigrams from the I, Ching, all written in golden ink. The mirror has many powers: • It shows the true form of anyone reflected in it, including shapeshifters, the undead, and those merely in disguise • It raises all difficulties for evil spirits and demons by one when it is present. For one Quintessence per minute, it forces such beings to stay at least three meters away without causing them pain. If they en- ter this area, then they will take one health level of damage per turn per point of the owner’s Arete for as long as they stay in the area. • It helps locate Feng Shui lines, giving a decrease in difficulty of two for all Feng Shui related rolls Other Beings 109 110 Mystic Armory Primal Energy Mages often live and die by their access to Quintes- sence. It’s only natural that they have devised ways to store excess Quintessence and otherwise manipulate the energies that are so essential to their very survival. Periapts This section consists of a collection of objects that can store Quintessence. Often, they impart a specific Resonance onto the Quintessence stored within it, making them most useful for specific applications. Advanced Power Cell (APC)

name: Twilight Balm
description: This balm is capable of curing almost any spiritual infection, toxin or poison. It is made from mandrake root, a clean linen and 47 herbs gathered at dawn. If it is applied under the light of the moon, it will work. Nature Most of the Wonders affecting the forces of nature do so by manipulating living things, though not all do. Among the Wonders in this section are those the con- trol animals, plants, fertility…but also the Pahu Ino- Nui which controls the weather and can summon ex- tremely violent storms. Cord of the Three Winds

name: Twins, The
description: These blades are believed to be the center of many myths involving pairs of swords, from the Eight Im- mortals to Muramasa. When destroyed, eventually these blades are recreated. One of the two is charged with Yang, the other with Yin, and the bearers of these blades are compelled to seek out ownership of the other. Of course, possessing both brings a horrible curse upon anyone who uses either blade. Defenses Mystic and technological defenses are common and useful. They include a lot of active defenses as well as just those that keep hostiles away through confusion or powerful warding. Daedalean Passages

name: TWURP
description: Computers 27 The Trusty Wrist-Unit Rote Processor is a wrist- watch computer, capable of storing contact infor- mation, schedule, sending and receiving text messages, etc. But, most importantly, it’s a miniaturized cousin of the Magickal Macro Keyboard . It can store up to four Virtual Adept rotes that are below the Master level (no spheres may be at 5), each can be used once. When used, the rotes are cast with the TWURP’s Arete and costs a point of Quintes- sence, though no further Quintessence may be used to decrease difficulty. Universal Nanotech Interface

name: Ultra-Silencer
description: An accessory for firearms that makes them complete- ly silent: the explosion that propels the bullet, the crack of the bullet breaking the sound barrier, even the action of the gun, are all converted to non-sound ener- gy and dispersed over a wide area. Unbullets

name: Unbullets
description: These are .44 caliber bullets with jade tips that, when shot from a pistol, heal the amount that normal bullets would damage. As they only come in .44 caliber size, the heal 6 dice of Lethal damage to a target. Being shot with one causes a burst of intense pleasure around the “wound.” Warware

name: Undead Strength
description: By installing the muscles from a Vampire, the Scien- tist may spend up to three Quintessence (and the same number of Health levels) to get that many automatic successes on Strength rolls for the scene. If Rejected, the relevant limb is useless until healed. Blessings Permanent Wonders aren’t just for Technocrats. Mystics create Blessings, usually in the form of magick- al tattoos but sometimes taking other forms. Aole Koheoheo Tatu

name: UniCash Card
description: Like the MIDAS Card, the UniCash Card can pull unlimited funds out of the system. Unlike the MIDAS Card, it is untraceable, even to the Syndicate. It dis- tributes its withdrawals to hundreds of accounts as "service charges" and they are ignored. They are, of course, extremely rare and only given to the most trusted Syndicate Associates. Universal ID

name: Universal ID
description: A simple ID card with an awakened spirit and some magicks to allow it to transform itself. It can become any sort of ID card imaginable, as needed. The user signs it with the name they want to use, and then touch the card to an example, image or video of the ID they need to replicate, and they will have a perfect replica. It will even give the user knowledge of passcodes needed to properly use the ID. UNIVID

name: Universal Identification Card Kit
description: A small, leather bound booklet with a microcomput- er, two business cards and a badge. The microcomput- er will configure the business cards and the badge to any identity that that Agent indicates with an Arete roll, difficulty 6. This will fool most people, but a Per- ception + Subterfuge or Law roll at difficulty 7 will allow someone to see through it. Computers 31 32 Mystic Armory Engineering and Materials Materials that go beyond the capabilities of mun- dane craftsman and scientists are often the stuff of legend. Engines of creation and destruction, alchemi- cal apparati, and just seemingly mundane objects that are objects of power all figure into these legends and have become common Wonders and Devices that Enlightened Alchemy is one of the oldest mystical sciences, and its descendent, Chemistry, has been popular and use- ful for centuries. Both the outputs and the tools used for these tightly related fields can be made or consid- ered to be magickal, and when they are, they are won- ders. Abundanti's Oil

name: Universal Suit
description: The single most common piece of Void Engineer equipment, the Universal Suit is always made out of Ionic Cloth and has attachments for further modules. They generally look like bulky jumpsuits, they can be more specifically engineered for those who need pro- tection from specific extreme environments. Wearables 173 Appendix: Optional Rules The rules given in various Mage supplements for Wonders often have to grapple with two conflicting things. On the one hand, wonders are discussed as rare and each one should have a significant story behind it before it shows up in a game. On the other hand, however, there are (as seen in the previous chapters) many wonders listed throughout the books as well as detailed rules for PCs to create their own. Another conflict is that though there is a desire to have PCs Wonder Wonder Spheres are a solution that first appeared in Tradition Book: Sons of Ether (Revised) on page 65. It gives a distinct advantage to Mages who build wonders as a matter of routine rather than fast-casting their effects on the fly. Under this system, a Mage who prefers this careful approach may purchase Spheres at half cost (in terms of either XP or freebie points), though always rounded up. Spheres bought in this way are considered to be understood well enough to create wonders, whether Charms, Artifacts or Talismans (or Gadgets, Inven- tions or Devices), but cannot be used in any other context: the understanding to put something together quickly with them just isn’t there. A Mage with create their own wonders and leave a permanent mark on Mage society, there is very little mechanical incen- tive in doing so, with many players finding wonders beyond Charms/Gadgets to be of little use to them. Here, we present some optional systems for incentiviz- ing wonder creation and explaining why Mages would put in the Herculean efforts necessary to create many of the wonders listen in this book. Spheres Wonder Sphere can only teach it to others (or inscribe it in a Grimoire) as a Wonder Sphere. Though there are no restrictions on how many of the Mage’s spheres may be Wonder Spheres, they can- not be raised above Arete, just like full Spheres, and they cannot be bought if the Mage has the Sphere Natural merit or the Sphere Inept Flaw in that area. Converting a Wonder Sphere into a full sphere costs a bit more than the difference in cost: 6 points for the first rank and 6 x Current Rating for each rank above the first. Of course, Wonder Sphere ranks must be converted in order, so that it is impossible to have a Wonder Sphere rank of 3 and a full Sphere rank of 4 a in any single area. Role-play-wise, this system fits best for Technomages with strong ties to their inventions, and particularly mystic or dynamic Mages would have trouble being satisfied with Wonder Spheres. This can be taken as a partial explanation for the success of the Technocracy: a large collection of relatively Static Mages who are particularly focused on learning Wonder Spheres ra- ther than full Spheres and building Wonders that give them defense against Paradox, advance knowledge of how powerful their effects will be, etc. would have a distinct advantage so long as they have planned for the situation they find themselves in. Mages who have Dynamic and Questing Avatars in particular, but also some others, may require that the Mage convert most Wonder Spheres to full Spheres before accomplishing a Seeking. Even with Wonder Spheres, the intense cost of cre- ating a Talisman is too much for many Mages to seri- ously consider, as doing so costs them a permanent point of Willpower. One possible incentive for a Mage to be willing to pay such a high cost is to make crafting more powerful Talismans easier. There are several ways to do this, among them are: • Talismans may have effects requiring Spheres one level higher than the Mage’s Spheres, including surpassing their Arete. This creates a situation where sometimes a loss of Willpower may be worth it to create an item of immense power. Of course, if the Mage should lose that item, it’s just more power in the hands of their enemies. • Willpower is only required for the first copy of a Talisman; future copies can be made without the cost.
